sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: QVC and HSN Streaming Service Launches on Comcast’s Xfinity X1 and Xfinity Flex
feed.summary: QVC and HSN have launched their streaming service on Comcast's Xfinity X1 and Xfinity Flex, making it the first video shopping app on either platform. The service provides X1 and Flex customers access to QVC and HSN's linear channels, on-demand video, and original programming, with plans to make the service shoppable by early 2022. This launch strengthens QVC and HSN's leadership in video commerce and expands their reach to new digital platforms.

feed.title: AT&T to run core 5G network on Microsoft's cloud
feed.summary: AT&T announced it will run core parts of its 5G wireless network on Microsoft's cloud computing platform, marking a significant win for Microsoft in the telecommunications sector. This partnership involves Microsoft purchasing software and intellectual property from AT&T and offering jobs to several hundred AT&T engineers. The move allows AT&T to leverage a larger ecosystem of software developers and will help Microsoft build its Azure for Operators product for telecommunications companies globally.

feed.title: Hardik Parekh, Former Head of Product Security at Splunk, Joins Five9 as Chief Information Security Officer
feed.summary: Five9, Inc. has appointed Hardik Parekh, formerly Head of Product Security at Splunk, as its new Chief Information Security Officer. Parekh brings extensive experience in developing world-class security programs from companies like Intuit, Amazon, and Splunk, and will be responsible for enhancing and executing Five9's global cybersecurity strategy. His appointment comes as Five9 continues to grow its enterprise business and expand internationally in the cloud contact center space.

feed.title: We Love These Air-Powered Half-Inch Drive Impact Wrenches
feed.summary: This article highlights the enduring power and utility of air-powered half-inch drive impact wrenches, despite the rise of cordless electric tools. It recommends several top models from brands like Ingersoll Rand, Aircat, and Campbell Hausfeld, detailing their key features such as torque, weight, and suggested use cases. The article emphasizes that pneumatic impact wrenches remain superior for raw nut-busting torque, making them essential tools for professional and serious DIY mechanics.

feed.title: HPE buys Israeli cloud data co Zerto for $374m
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has announced its acquisition of Israeli cloud data management and protection company Zerto for $374 million in cash. Zerto's technology, which includes disaster recovery, backup, and data mobility, will be integrated into HPE's GreenLake cloud data services. This acquisition is expected to contribute over $130 million in run-rate revenue to HPE.

feed.title: Amazon Adds New Leadership Principles Days Before Jeff Bezos Steps Down
feed.summary: Amazon has added two new "Leadership Principles" — "Strive to be Earth’s Best Employer" and "Success and Scale Bring Broad Responsibility" — just days before Jeff Bezos steps down as CEO. These new principles address long-standing criticisms regarding Amazon's workplace culture and its impact on society and the environment. They build on Bezos's earlier commitment to improve the company's working conditions and encourage employees to consider their colleagues' well-being and broader societal impact.

feed.title: HPE To Acquire Zerto For $374M To Boost GreenLake, Storage
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise is acquiring cloud data management and storage protection specialist Zerto for $374 million to enhance its HPE GreenLake cloud data services. This acquisition is expected to add over $130 million in run-rate revenue and will strengthen HPE's shift towards a cloud-native, software-defined data services business. Zerto's technology offers continuous data protection, disaster recovery, backup, and data mobility across various environments, and will be integrated into HPE GreenLake's as-a-service portfolio.

feed.title: Euro Tax Flash from KPMG's EU Tax Centre
feed.summary: KPMG's Euro Tax Flash reports on the historic agreement reached by 130 countries in the OECD/G20 Inclusive Framework on BEPS 2.0, aiming to reform international tax rules with a two-pillar approach. Pillar One reallocates taxing rights for large companies to market countries, while Pillar Two establishes a global minimum tax rate of at least 15%. The article also discusses potential EU implementation challenges and notes that some countries, like Ireland, have reservations about certain aspects of the agreement.

feed.title: Judge Releases Pharmacies and Retailers from Zantac Multidistrict Litigation
feed.summary: A federal judge in Florida has dismissed pharmacies and retailers, including Albertsons, Walgreens, CVS, and Amazon, from the Zantac multidistrict litigation. The judge ruled that consumers failed to provide sufficient evidence that these companies' negligence caused the formation of carcinogens in Zantac or that they improperly stored the drug. The lawsuit, which began in 2019 after the FDA found cancer-causing NDMA in Zantac, will continue against brand name manufacturers and generic-drug makers.

feed.title: Costco vs. Sam's Club vs. BJ's: Which wholesale club is best for you?
feed.summary: This article compares Costco, Sam's Club, and BJ's, helping consumers decide which wholesale club best suits their needs. It details membership fees, perks, and the number of U.S. locations for each, highlighting how wholesale clubs offer lower markups compared to traditional retailers. The analysis aims to show if the annual membership fees are justified by the potential savings and benefits.

feed.title: HPE to Acquire Zerto, a Cloud Data Management and Protection Company, Bolstering GreenLake Storage Portfolio
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) announced its acquisition of Zerto, a cloud data management and protection company, for $374 million. This acquisition is set to enhance HPE's GreenLake portfolio, specifically strengthening its cloud-native, software-defined storage services. Zerto's continuous data protection technology for disaster recovery and ransomware protection across hybrid and multi-cloud environments will significantly contribute to HPE's offerings and competitiveness in the rapidly growing data protection as a service market.

feed.title: Predictive maintenance for drones using embedded ML
feed.summary: This article details a project on smart predictive maintenance for drones using embedded machine learning and the QuickLogic QuickFeather development kit. The solution aims to detect minute flaws in drone components, such as loose screws or broken propellers, by analyzing sound data, to prevent catastrophic failures and facilitate efficient autonomous last-mile delivery. The project underscores the importance of transitioning to electric vehicles and proactive maintenance for future drone and UGV-based delivery systems.

feed.title: Shinsegae Acquires eBay Korea In $3 Billion Deal As E-Commerce Booms
feed.summary: South Korean retail giant Shinsegae, through its supermarket affiliate E-Mart, has acquired eBay Korea for $3 billion. This strategic move aims to boost Shinsegae's market share in Korea's rapidly growing e-commerce sector and enhance its delivery and warehouse capabilities. The acquisition positions Shinsegae as a major player against competitors like Naver and Coupang, with plans to invest further in fulfillment services.

feed.title: Zebra Technologies to buy warehouse automation startup Fetch Robotics
feed.summary: Zebra Technologies announced its acquisition of Fetch Robotics for $290 million, a move expected to accelerate its focus on intelligent industrial automation and expand its presence in the robotics sector. Fetch Robotics specializes in Autonomous Mobile Robots (AMRs) for warehouse operations, and this acquisition aims to help retailers compete with Amazon's automated systems. The deal highlights Zebra's strategy to integrate factory automation with supply chain solutions and enhance efficiency for its customers.

feed.title: Sandisk Professional G-Drive rugged external 2TB SSD review
feed.summary: The Sandisk Professional G-Drive is a rugged, external 2TB SSD designed for durability and high-speed transfers. While expensive, it offers IP67 water and dust resistance, 3-meter drop protection, and good performance with transfer speeds up to 1GBps. The drive comes with a five-year warranty and targets professionals needing physically secure data storage.

feed.title: How to Get Roadside Assistance Using Alexa
feed.summary: This article explains how to use Amazon Alexa to request roadside assistance through various third-party apps like Urgent.ly, AAA, and Blink Roadside. It details the setup process for each service, including enabling skills, granting permissions, and the specific voice commands to get help. The author recommends Blink Roadside for its ease of use, cost-effectiveness, and lack of membership requirements.

feed.title: Amazon stock nabs record after Bezos-Jassy handoff
feed.summary: Amazon's stock reached an all-time high of $3,675.74 after Andy Jassy officially took over as CEO from Jeff Bezos. Investors appear confident in Jassy, who previously ran Amazon Web Services, as the company nears a $2 trillion market capitalization, aiming to join Apple and Microsoft. Jassy also received a substantial incentive package of 61,000 shares, valued at over $214 million, vesting over 10 years.

feed.title: Zevia IPO: 5 things to know about the zero-calorie beverage company before it goes public
feed.summary: Zevia PBC, a beverage company specializing in zero-calorie, zero-sugar drinks, has filed to go public. The company, which was the top-selling carbonated soft drink brand on Amazon in 2020, plans to list its class A shares on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker "ZVIA."

feed.title: Founder Of Turkish E-Commerce Firm Hepsiburada A Billionaire After IPO
feed.summary: Hanzade Dogan-Boyner, founder of Turkish e-commerce giant Hepsiburada, became a billionaire after the company's U.S. Nasdaq debut. Her stake in the company and other investments brought her net worth to an estimated $1.5 billion, making her one of five women billionaires in Turkey. Hepsiburada, often dubbed Turkey's Amazon, saw its stock rise on its first trading day.

feed.title: 'Abuse of dominance': Was this what Congress originally intended for Section 2?
feed.summary: The article discusses a potential shift in U.S. antitrust enforcement, moving toward stricter policing of single-firm dominance, akin to the European "abuse of dominance" standard. This change is influenced by Senator Amy Klobuchar's proposed legislation and President Biden's appointment of Lina M. Khan to the FTC, reflecting concerns about the market power of technology giants. The author argues that this renewed approach to antitrust law may align more closely with the original intent of the Sherman Act, which historically sought to prevent concentration of power.

feed.title: LG swings battle for premium TV supremacy
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic has accelerated demand for premium TVs, with OLED TV shipments increasing significantly. LG Electronics has seen its share of the premium TV market rise to 28% by Q1, while Samsung's share declined. LG Display's OLED technology is driving this shift, with confidence in OLED's image quality, eye health benefits, and environmental advantages leading to continued market growth and diversification among TV manufacturers.

feed.title: From Amazon to Starbucks, What Companies Paid Workers in the Pandemic
feed.summary: A Wall Street Journal analysis of S&P 500 companies revealed how median worker pay was impacted during the Covid-19 pandemic. While many saw their salaries largely unchanged or rise, nearly 140 companies paid their median worker at least $100,000, and four dozen, including Starbucks and Amazon, paid less than $30,000. These findings highlight the varied financial experiences of employees across different sectors during the pandemic.

feed.title: New Walmart+ Head Hired
feed.summary: Walmart Inc. has hired Chris Cracchiolo, formerly of American Express, to lead its membership program Walmart+. Cracchiolo will officially start as SVP and general manager of Walmart+ on July 12, replacing David Echegoyen. Walmart+ currently boasts nearly 12 million members and offers benefits like free home delivery, fuel discounts, and discounted prescriptions.

feed.title: Digital Pharmacies Filling Orders Online Go After CVS, Walgreens
feed.summary: Digital pharmacies are emerging as disruptors to traditional brick-and-mortar chains like CVS and Walgreens, promising to save customers time, money, and hassle. Startups like Alto, Capsule, and Medly have collectively raised significant capital, with Amazon's acquisition of PillPack further validating the nascent sector. These companies aim to streamline the prescription fulfillment process, targeting a substantial U.S. industry.

feed.title: Ford lures top lawyer, senior Amazon employee in latest high-profile hires
feed.summary: Ford Motor Co. has announced two high-profile hires, Steven Croley as chief policy officer and general counsel, and Rebecca Pagani as chief privacy officer. These appointments reflect Ford's commitment to strengthening its government relations, sustainability, legal, and privacy matters, particularly as the company pivots towards electrification. Croley brings extensive regulatory experience from his previous role as general counsel for the U.S. Department of Energy and his time in the Obama administration, while Pagani joins from Amazon with expertise in information security and privacy laws for e-commerce, cloud computing, and AI businesses.

feed.title: Trump sues Facebook, Twitter and Google over social media bans
authors: Mark Niquette and Naomi Nix and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Former US President Donald Trump has filed class-action lawsuits against Facebook, Twitter, and Google, as well as their chief executives, aiming to overturn his social media bans and seek punitive damages. Trump argues that these tech giants, by banning him, are violating First Amendment rights and should be considered government actors due to their liability protections under Section 230. The lawsuits follow his permanent ban from Twitter and a two-year suspension from Facebook after the January 6 Capitol riot, reigniting debates about Section 230 and social media censorship of conservative viewpoints.

feed.title: Why Bloomingdale's is launching a chain of smaller stores
feed.summary: Bloomingdale's is launching a new chain of smaller stores called "Bloomie's," with the first location opening in Fairfax, Virginia, on August 26th. These stores will be approximately one-seventh the size of regular Bloomingdale's, focusing on trendy brands and quicker merchandise turnover, and will not be located in traditional malls. The initiative aims to adapt to changing shopper habits, attract new customers, especially younger ones, and create a more dynamic and accessible retail experience, much like its sister company Macy's.

feed.title: Oracle CEO Safra Catz underscores Israel support in Jerusalem data center launch
feed.summary: Oracle CEO Safra Catz emphasized Oracle's strong support for Israel during the launch of a new data center in Jerusalem, despite the company losing a major cloud project tender to AWS and Google. Catz, an Israeli-born executive, stated that Oracle's commitment to Israel is unwavering and that employees who disagree with this mission might not be a fit for the company. She also expressed confusion regarding Oracle not being selected for Israel's $1 billion "Nimbus" cloud project.

feed.title: American Express expands Digital Receipts with increased purchase details
feed.summary: American Express has enhanced its Digital Receipts feature to provide Card Members shopping on Amazon with more detailed purchase information, including specific items, order numbers, and delivery details. This expansion builds on the Digital Receipts solution rolled out earlier this year, which offers Card Members additional purchase data from select merchants. The initiative, in partnership with Verifi, aims to increase transparency and convenience for customers.

feed.title: American Express rolls out ‘Digital Receipts’ feature for Amazon purchases
feed.summary: American Express is expanding its Digital Receipts initiative to include Amazon purchases, providing cardholders with detailed transaction information like order numbers and delivery details. This expansion follows a survey where consumers indicated that digital receipts would reduce charge disputes and help differentiate between fraudulent and legitimate charges. Merchants also believe this feature will improve customer experience and minimize unnecessary chargebacks.

feed.title: Employers offer stipends for babysitting and tutoring to sweeten child-care benefits and win over workers
feed.summary: Companies are increasingly offering enhanced child-care benefits, including backup care, stipends for tutoring and babysitting, and even college counseling, to attract and retain employees, especially working parents. This trend emerged during the Covid-19 pandemic as employers recognized the need to support workers with caregiving responsibilities, aiming to bring women back into the workforce and improve overall employee well-being. These expanded benefits also extend to support for older children and elderly family members, reflecting a broader shift in employer-employee relationships.

feed.title: JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NYSEARCA:JUSA)
feed.summary: The JPMorgan U.S. Research Enhanced Large Cap ETF (JUSA) is an actively managed ETF focusing on large-cap US equities, launched on July 7, 2021, and managed by JPMorgan Chase. The ETF has seen a 7.6% increase in its stock price since the beginning of the year and currently has $204.68 million in assets under management. Its top holdings include major technology companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon.com.

feed.title: What McDonald’s minimum wage raise says about fast-food franchise future
feed.summary: McDonald's is raising wages for employees at its company-owned restaurants to an average of $15 per hour by 2024, responding to a tight labor market and competition for workers. This move puts pressure on its 95% franchised restaurants to follow suit, with some franchise owners already seeing higher retention rates after implementing similar wage increases. The decision highlights a shift in the fast-food industry towards higher pay, influenced by advocacy groups and a strong economy.

feed.title: Reddit Upvotes The Land Down Under
feed.summary: Reddit has officially launched its Australian operations with a new office in Sydney, recognizing Australia as its fourth-largest user base and a key part of its international growth strategy. The new team includes dedicated Community, Engineering, Sales staff, and Country Manager David Ray, aiming to build on strong organic user growth and establish local brand partnerships. Australian Reddit users are highly engaged, spending more time on the platform than on Instagram, Snapchat, Twitter, and Pinterest, and show popularity for local communities alongside global trends.

feed.title: GM turns to supplier to build initial EV vans while it readies plant in Canada
feed.summary: General Motors is utilizing German supplier Kuka AG to produce the initial, small batch of its new EV600 commercial vans to accelerate deliveries to customers like Fedex. This atypical move allows GM to meet its late 2021 rollout target, as its Canadian plant designated for EV van production is still manufacturing Chevrolet Equinox SUVs and won't be ready until late 2022. This strategy highlights GM's urgency to enter the growing electric commercial van market.

feed.title: Casey’s General Stores Supply Chain Team Contributes to Strong Financial Results
feed.summary: Casey's General Stores, an "essential" convenience store chain serving smaller towns, has achieved strong financial results despite the challenging retail environment and a decline in fuel sales during the pandemic. The company's self-distribution model, a new $60 million distribution center in Joplin, Missouri, and technological advancements like pick-to-light machines and a Softeon WMS have significantly enhanced its supply chain efficiency. Additionally, Casey's expanded into curbside pickups and home deliveries via DoorDash and Uber Eats, supported by a centralized procurement team, further contributing to its success.

feed.title: Tejon Ranch Commerce Center Draws Tenant Interest
feed.summary: The Tejon Ranch Commerce Center is seeing significant tenant interest due to record absorption, limited inventory, and its strategic location offering lower costs compared to the L.A. basin. Its ability to serve a large consumer base and offer space for "safety stock" has attracted multi-national companies. Recent speculative buildings developed in partnership with Majestic and Tejon Ranch Co. have been fully leased, indicating strong market demand.

feed.title: Defective LG Washing Machine Caused Thousands in Damage, Claims Insurer’s $100K Lawsuit
feed.summary: Allstate Insurance Company has filed a $100,000 lawsuit against LG Electronics USA, Inc., alleging a defective LG washing machine caused significant water damage to an Alabama home. The lawsuit claims LG sold a dangerous product, model WM2487HRMA, with a known defect in its control board, leading to a "sudden and uncontrolled discharge of water." Allstate is seeking $99,005.48 for repairs and damages, along with attorney and court fees, accusing LG of negligence, failure to warn, breach of warranty, and violating consumer protection law.

feed.title: ZoomInfo acquires Chorus.ai for $575M to expand its sales intelligence platform
feed.summary: ZoomInfo Technologies Inc. has announced the acquisition of sales analytics startup Chorus.ai Inc. for $575 million. Chorus.ai uses AI to analyze sales conversations, providing insights to improve sales team productivity and detect market changes. This acquisition is part of ZoomInfo's strategy to expand its sales technology market presence and enhance its go-to-market platform.

feed.title: Ford hires general counsel and policy chief
feed.summary: Ford Motor Company has appointed Steven Croley as its new chief policy officer and general counsel, effective July 12. Croley, previously a partner at Latham & Watkins and former general counsel for the US Department of Energy, will report to CEO Jim Farley. He replaces John Mellen, who will retire in September after transitioning his legal responsibilities.

feed.title: A complete guide to Amazon stock
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to Amazon stock, discussing its performance, market position, and how investors can trade it. It highlights Amazon's primary revenue streams (retail, subscriptions, and AWS), the company's valuation, and key factors investors should consider before trading AMZN shares, such as its growth-over-profit strategy and the importance of AWS.

feed.title: How Sonoco used Oracle Cloud as part of its multicloud tech transformation
feed.summary: Sonoco, a packaging company, embarked on a multicloud strategy to modernize its IT infrastructure, moving legacy applications from on-premises data centers to the cloud. The company leveraged Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for its Oracle databases and applications, taking advantage of the Oracle-Microsoft interconnect for seamless integration with Azure. This transition allowed Sonoco to build purpose-built cloud environments, scale resources efficiently, and enhance disaster recovery and security for its operations.

feed.title: Why is Tesla incorporated in Delaware?
feed.summary: This article explains why a disproportionate number of companies, including Tesla, Amazon, and Google, are incorporated in Delaware despite not being headquartered there. Key reasons include flexible corporate law, the efficient Court of Chancery, favorable tax treatment for out-of-state income, and a long-standing state focus on business formation. The article also touches on the challenges this presents for regional data and business coverage.

feed.title: Self-Driving Startup Aurora Set to Go Public
feed.summary: Aurora Innovation Inc., a self-driving technology startup, has agreed to go public through a merger with Reinvent Technology Partners Y, a special purpose acquisition company. The deal values Aurora at $11 billion and the combined entity at $13 billion, providing about $2.5 billion in cash to commercialize self-driving trucks. The company, led by CEO Chris Urmson, plans to initially focus on the trucking market.

feed.title: Lionsgate Acquires Spyglass Media Stake In Broad Strategic Partnership
feed.summary: Lionsgate has acquired a 20% investment stake in Spyglass Media Group and the vast majority of its 200-title feature film library, forming a strategic content partnership. The agreement includes a multiyear first-look television deal between Lionsgate Television and Spyglass. Gary Barber will continue to lead Spyglass as its Chairman and CEO, with the deal aiming to grow Lionsgate's IP portfolio and global content distribution platform.

feed.title: Lionsgate Nabs About 200 Films From Spyglass Media in Stake Deal
feed.summary: Lionsgate has acquired a 20 percent stake in Spyglass Media Group, along with approximately 200 film titles from Spyglass's library. This strategic investment aims to bolster Lionsgate's content portfolio, especially for its streaming service Starz, and positions the company for further consolidation in the entertainment industry. Gary Barber will continue to lead Spyglass as chairman and CEO, while both companies look to jointly create new content.

feed.title: Lionsgate Acquires Bulk of Weinstein Film Library in Spyglass Deal
feed.summary: Lionsgate has acquired the bulk of the Weinstein Co.'s feature film library from Spyglass Media as part of a broader partnership. This deal includes acclaimed films like "Django Unchained," "Inglourious Basterds," and "The King's Speech" and gives Lionsgate a 20% equity stake in Spyglass, while Spyglass gains a first-look TV deal with Lionsgate. The acquisition significantly expands Lionsgate's content library and provides Spyglass with capital for new projects.

feed.title: PACCAR, Volvo invest in self-driving tech company Aurora’s $10.6B SPAC
feed.summary: PACCAR and Volvo Group have invested in Aurora Innovation, a self-driving technology company, which is going public through a SPAC merger with a $10.6 billion enterprise valuation. Aurora plans to launch its first Level 4 autonomous vehicle by the end of 2023, aiming to commercialize self-driving technology at scale for the U.S. trucking and passenger transportation markets. The deal includes significant investments from the Reinvent SPAC, existing Aurora shareholders, and other investors, with protective measures like share price vesting and lockup periods for founders' shares.

feed.title: Target's teacher discount is coming back. Here's how educators, including home school teachers can save 15%
feed.summary: Target is bringing back its 15% back-to-school teacher discount for two weeks, running from July 18 to July 31. This discount is available to various educators, including K-12, homeschool, preschool teachers, and university professors, who verify their eligibility through the Target Circle loyalty program. Target also announced a separate discount for college students, offering $15 off for every $50 spent on household essentials from August 15-21, and a 15% off one-time discount for new Target Circle student members.

feed.title: The Costs of Digitizing Education
feed.summary: The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the costs and challenges of digitizing education, revealing dissatisfaction with online learning outcomes and increased expenses. However, the author argues for a broader perspective, suggesting that technology can improve both educational outcomes and economics by addressing systematic issues in traditional education. Companies like John Wiley & Sons, New Oriental Education, Pearson, and TAL Education are positioned to capitalize on these trends through credentialing, online programs, and innovative content delivery models.

feed.title: Movie Theater Stocks Take Hit Amid Rising COVID-19 Cases, Streaming Uncertainty
feed.summary: Movie theater stocks, including AMC, Cinemark, and Imax, experienced a significant sell-off due to rising concerns over the Delta variant of COVID-19 and Disney's disclosure of $60 million in Premier Access revenue for "Black Widow." Analysts noted that investor sentiment was affected by both macro-driven fears about the pandemic's impact on reopening businesses and anxieties about the future of theatrical distribution amidst increasing premium video-on-demand releases. Despite the downturn, some analysts believe the market is overlooking factors such as revenue sharing for PVOD platforms and piracy issues, suggesting that strong theatrical demand still exists.

feed.title: Beware of the Sugar Rush: Thoughts on the Google Chrome 3rd Party Cookie Announcement
feed.summary: Google's decision to delay the sunsetting of third-party cookies in Chrome should not lead to complacency for marketers and media owners. Instead, this extra time should be utilized to develop robust first-party data strategies and enhance contextual targeting solutions. The article emphasizes that a cookieless future is inevitable and acting now will create a more sustainable and trustworthy online ecosystem.

feed.title: Putting the ‘Q’ in NASDAQ: Quantum Computing Inc. Lists on Nasdaq Capital Market
feed.summary: Quantum Computing Inc. (QCI) has announced its listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market, with common stock trading under the symbol QUBT set to commence on July 15, 2021. QCI aims to democratize quantum computing with its Qatalyst™ software, enabling businesses to solve complex problems without extensive quantum programming expertise. This Nasdaq listing is expected to boost QCI's profile, liquidity, and shareholder value in the investment community.

feed.title: I just sold my home to Redfin. Did I make a deal with the devil?
feed.summary: The author recounts his experience selling his Los Angeles home to iBuyer Redfin, highlighting the convenience and financial benefits, especially during the pandemic. He evaluates the potential broader impacts of such tech-driven real estate transactions on the housing market and traditional real estate agents. The article concludes by questioning whether this trend, despite immediate advantages, could lead to larger monopolistic issues in the industry.

feed.title: What became of 2020's biggest US tech IPOs?
feed.summary: This article reviews the performance of the three biggest US tech IPOs of 2020 (Snowflake, DoorDash, and Airbnb) since their public debuts. It details their IPO specifics, what they do, and their stock performance up to July 16, 2021, highlighting their initial surges and subsequent fluctuations. The article also touches upon the upcoming tech IPOs in India and other significant tech-related news from the week.

feed.title: The Competitive Landscape in the BNPL Sector
feed.summary: This article discusses the intensifying competitive landscape within the Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) sector, highlighted by Apple's recent entry. It traces the history of installment plans, analyzes how BNPL providers like Afterpay and Humm Group are navigating increasing competition from major financial institutions and tech giants, and examines their strategies for profitability and differentiation amidst growing regulatory scrutiny and declining valuations.

feed.title: It's ‘very dangerous’ to invest in stocks and bitcoin right now, long-time bear David Tice warns
feed.summary: Long-time bear David Tice, known for his bearish bets, warns that investing in stocks and bitcoin is "very dangerous" due to an overvalued market, rising debt, and strange treasury market behavior. He suggests investors avoid pursuing small gains that risk a significant pullback, especially in Big Tech and FAANG stocks. Tice, who turned bearish on bitcoin, now advocates for investing in gold and silver mining companies, believing they offer protection against fiat money and significant growth opportunities.

feed.title: Rivian delays much-hyped electric bakkie
feed.summary: Rivian Automotive Inc. is delaying the production of its electric pickup truck, the R1T, by two months to September, and its electric SUV, the R1S, from August to an unspecified time in the fall. These delays are primarily due to supply-chain bottlenecks, particularly a semiconductor shortage, which has broadly impacted the automotive industry. CEO R.J. Scaringe noted the compounding effects of the pandemic on facility construction, equipment installation, and component supply.

feed.title: The Zoom-Five9 deal is a big bet for the video conferencing company
feed.summary: Zoom has announced its intent to acquire Five9 for $14.7 billion, representing nearly 15% of Zoom's market capitalization. This acquisition is seen as a significant bet for Zoom to expand its offerings beyond video conferencing and tap into Five9's customer base, despite Five9's slower revenue growth compared to Zoom's core business. The deal aims to enhance total addressable market and create accretive value for both companies.

feed.title: Focus: Getting the cap on the bottle: Inside P&G's robot ambitions
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (P&G) is using advanced robotics and automation to maintain its competitive edge in the consumer goods market by reducing manufacturing costs and improving efficiency. Despite being known for branding, P&G develops its own automation solutions, focusing on tasks like precise packaging and handling diverse product shapes. This push for automation is also driven by the need for social distancing during the pandemic and labor shortages, with P&G continuously exploring new robotic applications for its factories.

feed.title: Capital One Steps Up Hiring Software Engineers After Cloud Migration
feed.summary: Capital One is significantly increasing its hiring of software engineers, aiming to add over 3,000 technologists by the end of the year. This hiring surge is partly attributed to the bank's migration to Amazon's public cloud, which is being used as a recruitment incentive for new employees. Mike Eason, SVP of engineering at Capital One, notes that engineers are drawn to working in cloud environments.

feed.title: The Best Wireless Home Security Systems Are Easier to Set Up Than You Think
feed.summary: This article reviews five top wireless home security systems, highlighting their ease of setup and key features. It provides recommendations for different needs and budgets, from the top pick Ring Alarm Home Security System to budget-friendly options and systems for subscription-averse buyers. The article also discusses important considerations like the number of sensors and cameras needed, expansion options, and monthly service fees.

feed.title: Opioid maker J&J, distributors near $26B deal with US states
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson and three major drug distributors are finalizing a $26 billion settlement to resolve thousands of opioid-related lawsuits across the US. The deal involves McKesson Corp, Cardinal Health Inc, AmerisourceBergen Corp paying $21 billion and J&J contributing $5 billion. States have the option to accept the full settlement or parts of it, with bonuses for broader local government participation.

feed.title: 13 Best Server Security Services
feed.summary: This article reviews 13 top server security services, highlighting the critical importance of server protection against hackers and ransomware. It details various solutions, including firewalls, incident response software, vulnerability scanning, secure connectivity, cloud data protection, site recovery, and VPNs. The services aim to protect databases, confidential information, and enterprise user files, adapting to the increasing virtualization of servers.

feed.title: Revamped plans for entertainment district at Nashville Yards still feature AEG, and this partner taking MGM's place
feed.summary: AEG has announced revamped plans for the entertainment district at Nashville Yards, which will still include AEG but with a new partner replacing MGM. The updated development will feature changes from the original vision pursued by Los Angeles-based Anschutz Entertainment Group since they acquired the property over three years ago. The article details how the plans have evolved and identifies the new key partner.

feed.title: 4 best practices for big data security in cloud computing
feed.summary: Securing big data in the cloud is crucial due to the vast amounts of sensitive information involved. This article outlines four best practices for big data security in cloud computing, including architectural design, least privilege tactics, data encryption, and continuous monitoring. These strategies help businesses protect data integrity, prevent theft, and ensure regulatory compliance in cloud environments.

feed.title: Formula One TV Ratings Up Big Amid Liberty Media Turnaround
feed.summary: Formula One's TV ratings have significantly increased, particularly in the U.S., under Liberty Media's ownership, validating their modern approach to managing the sport. This growth is attracting investor interest and is expected to lead to substantial increases in broadcast, advertising, and sponsorship revenues, although some experts temper expectations for rights fee increases. The Netflix docuseries "Drive to Survive" and relaxed social media guidelines are credited with boosting F1's popularity and brand awareness.

feed.title: Kotak Global Innovation Fund of Fund Review
feed.summary: The Kotak Global Innovation Fund of Fund, which invests in the Wellington Global Innovation Fund, is reviewed in this article. The author advises investors to avoid this fund despite its recent outperformance due to high expense ratios and the fund's short history, suggesting alternatives like the Motilal Oswal S&P 500 Index Fund for international market exposure. The fund invests in global companies driving innovation and structural growth trends.

feed.title: Amazon workers must be paid for security checks - Penn. top court
feed.summary: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court ruled that Amazon must pay warehouse workers for time spent in post-shift security screenings, stating that state wage law is broader than federal law. The court found that "hours worked" includes any time workers are required to be on the employer's premises, regardless of whether it's a "de minimis" amount of time. This decision contrasts with federal law, which does not require such pay as security checks are deemed incidental to job duties.

feed.title: Common joins National Landing Community, Selected to Manage the Coliving Apartments at 2221 S. Clark St.
feed.summary: Common, a residential brand known for designing and managing coliving apartments, is expanding into National Landing, a mixed-use urban center in Arlington, Va. JBG SMITH selected Common to manage the 216-unit coliving apartments at 2221 S. Clark St., now named Common at National Landing, offering attainable housing starting below $1,000 per month. This move enhances housing options in National Landing, a rapidly developing area home to Amazon's HQ2 and Virginia Tech's innovation campus.

feed.title: Startup Couriers Snatch Toehold in Biggest Shake-Up of FedEx Era
feed.summary: The surge in e-commerce is overwhelming traditional delivery giants, leading to logistical bottlenecks and customer frustration. This environment is fostering the growth of innovative startup couriers, such as Veho Tech Inc., which utilize crowdsourced networks and gig drivers to provide faster delivery solutions. These new entrants are using technology like smartphones to optimize package delivery and meet next-day deadlines.

feed.title: Microsoft acquires security start-up CloudKnox
feed.summary: Microsoft announced its acquisition of CloudKnox, a security start-up that develops software to help companies manage and reduce access permissions to their cloud resources. This acquisition is part of Microsoft's broader strategy to expand its security business, which has seen over $10 billion in revenue in the prior 12 months. CloudKnox's software functions across Microsoft Azure, Amazon, and Google public clouds, identifying and removing unused permissions and flagging unusual activity to prevent security breaches.

feed.title: Varun Anand's Top Picks: July 21, 2021
feed.summary: Varun Anand, Vice President and Senior Portfolio Manager at Starlight Capital, shares his top infrastructure stock picks for July 21, 2021, emphasizing the sector's resilience amid global economic improvements and the delta variant surge. He highlights the potential benefits from President Biden's infrastructure bill and the increasing focus on decarbonization. His current top picks include Canadian National Railway (CNR TSX), RWE AG (RWE ETR), and Cargojet (CJT TSX).

feed.title: Cox Media Group Makes Deal With Amazon Advertising
feed.summary: Cox Media Group (CMG) has partnered with Amazon Advertising, allowing local advertisers working with CMG Local Solutions to buy and analyze display and over-the-top ads through the Amazon demand-side platform. This collaboration aims to provide local businesses with enhanced tools and expertise to achieve their marketing goals more effectively, building on a similar arrangement Amazon made with E.W. Scripps.

feed.title: J&J and three drug distributors unveil $26-billion opioid deal with states
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson and three major drug distributors (McKesson Corp., Cardinal Health Inc., and AmerisourceBergen Corp.) have reached a $26 billion settlement with states and local governments to resolve thousands of lawsuits related to the opioid crisis. The deal aims to provide funds for treatment and recovery services and address allegations of illegal marketing and negligence in handling addictive painkillers. While many states are expected to join the settlement, some, like Washington, have rejected the offer as insufficient.

feed.title: How an Investor Helped Netflix Disclose Its ESG Performance
feed.summary: Individual investor Lily Bowles successfully prompted Netflix to disclose its material ESG performance through a shareholder resolution, which she later withdrew after the company demonstrated willingness to comply. Her experience highlights how difficult it is for individual investors to engage with companies, especially with new SEC restrictions that make such efforts harder to replicate despite the potential for positive change. The article also discusses the potential for rule changes under the new SEC chairperson to mitigate these challenges.

feed.title: Leases to non-Amazon e-commerce companies triple for Prologis as online retail diversifies
feed.summary: Prologis saw its leases to non-Amazon e-commerce companies triple year-over-year in the first half of 2021, indicating a diversification and growth in the online retail sector beyond Amazon. This surge in demand for warehouse and distribution space is driven by the acceleration of online shopping due to the pandemic and companies needing to restock low inventories to meet increased consumer spending. Key markets like Southern California and New Jersey are experiencing particularly high demand and low vacancy rates for industrial real estate.

feed.title: Karleki, MIT Undergrad Startup, Launches After Raising $450K of Venture Capital from Cronos Ventures
feed.summary: Karleki, an internet tech platform founded by MIT student Jason Tang, has launched after securing $450K in pre-seed venture capital from Cronos Ventures. The startup aims to revolutionize business search by creating a platform where companies can raise money and gain publicity, acting as an investment bank for startups. Karleki differentiates itself by offering direct access to venture capitalist contact information, breaking down traditional barriers for entrepreneurs seeking investment.

feed.title: Worldwide internet outages: Major apps including Amazon, HBO Max, Airbnb down
feed.summary: A worldwide internet outage affected numerous major apps and websites, including Amazon, HBO Max, Airbnb, FedEx, and Delta Airlines. Akamai Technologies, a global content delivery network, confirmed a service disruption that caused the widespread issues. The company later implemented a fix, and services began resuming normal operations.

feed.title: The Mercedes-Benz Superdome is Renamed Caesars Superdome
feed.summary: The historic Mercedes-Benz Superdome has officially been renamed the Caesars Superdome after Louisiana lawmakers approved a 10-year, $138 million naming rights deal with Caesars Entertainment. This renaming follows a long process that included the New Orleans Saints hiring Oak View Group to secure a new naming sponsor and is part of a larger $450 million renovation project aimed at modernizing the facility and keeping the Saints in Louisiana until 2035.

feed.title: Chipotle, Ally jump in as Twitch expands sponsorship opportunities
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill and Ally Financial have become the first official marketing partners for /twitchgaming, signaling Twitch's expansion as a marketing platform beyond competitive gaming. These partnerships represent ongoing commitments rather than one-off campaigns, with Chipotle aiming to reach Gen Z and millennials for delivery orders and Ally sponsoring a series highlighting underrepresented groups in gaming. This move reflects the growing demand for creator content and Twitch's increased viewership, especially among gamers who are difficult to reach through traditional media.

feed.title: Editor’s Choice: Globalstar SPOT Gen4 satellite tracker
feed.summary: The Globalstar SPOT Gen4 satellite tracker is a rugged, off-grid device designed for safety in remote areas, succeeding the Gen3 model with minor updates like improved waterproofing and mapping software. It offers SOS, tracking, and messaging functions via satellite, requiring a monthly plan starting at £10, and is particularly useful for bluewater cruising or venturing beyond mobile phone signal range. The device has a 4/5 MBY rating and is considered an affordable safety net for boaters.

feed.title: Common Expands Washington, DC Co-Living Presence
feed.summary: Common has expanded its co-living presence in Washington, D.C., securing its first management assignment in the National Landing submarket at 2221 S. Clark St. JBG SMITH selected Common to manage the co-living units and studios of the 216-unit community. This addition brings Common's total operations in Washington, D.C. to nine properties, further growing its portfolio through new management agreements and acquisitions in other major cities.

feed.title: Rackspace Layoffs, Offshore Strategy Buried in 'Growth' Announcement -
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology announced plans to invest up to $75 million in growth, while simultaneously conducting layoffs affecting 10% of its workforce and shifting job responsibilities offshore. The company's press release focused on growth and financial guidance, but an SEC filing revealed the "termination" of employees and the backfilling of roles in offshore service centers. This led to criticism of Rackspace's "overly ambitious PR spin" for obscuring the job cuts despite increasing financial forecasts.

feed.title: Young Indian investors warming up to clean energy ETFs: Report
feed.summary: A report by Globalise indicates that young Indian investors are increasingly interested in clean energy ETFs, alongside growth-oriented technology funds. The ARK Innovation ETF, Renaissance IPO ETF, Small Cap Growth Vanguard ETF, Invesco WilderHill Clean Energy ETF, and Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 were among the top ETFs in demand. Young investors, particularly from states like Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Delhi, and Karnataka, are seeking to diversify their portfolios through global investing platforms.

feed.title: Amazon, Airbnb, UPS among major firms to face brief outage; Akamai says issues fixed | World News
feed.summary: Major companies including Amazon, Airbnb, and UPS experienced a brief service outage due to a disruption from Akamai Technologies, a web content delivery provider. Akamai quickly implemented a fix, and services rapidly resumed normal operations. The disruption also affected Indian companies like Zomato and Paytm.

feed.title: Taboola is Acquiring Connexity For $800M, Bringing e-Commerce Recommendations To The Open Web
feed.summary: Taboola has announced its acquisition of Connexity for $800 million, aiming to integrate e-commerce recommendations across the open web. This move is driven by Taboola's vision to expand into the e-commerce market and empower merchants and publishers outside of "walled gardens" like Amazon. The acquisition combines Connexity's extensive network of retailers and publishers with Taboola's recommendation technology to create a more diverse and e-commerce-driven open web experience.

feed.title: Supply-Chain Backlogs Turn Chicago Into New Chokepoint
feed.summary: Chicago has become a new bottleneck in the global supply chain due to a surge of imports from Asia. Railroads like Union Pacific and BNSF have restricted container shipments into their Chicago terminals, forcing some cargo owners to divert shipments to other Midwestern points, increasing costs and complicating logistics. This issue extends the supply-chain problems initially seen with container ships backed up at West Coast ports.

feed.title: Better Buy: Invesco QQQ or Fidelity MSCI IT Index ETF
feed.summary: This article compares the Invesco QQQ and Fidelity MSCI IT Index ETF, both strong technology-focused ETFs. While QQQ tracks the Nasdaq 100 and holds diverse sectors, the Fidelity ETF is a pure-play IT fund with broader diversification within the tech sector. The author concludes that the Fidelity ETF is the better buy due to its higher historical returns, lower expense ratio, and more consistent performance.

feed.title: Tobi Lutke becomes Canada’s wealthiest company founder as Shopify shares soar
feed.summary: Tobi Lutke, founder and CEO of Shopify Inc., has become Canada's wealthiest company founder as his stake in the e-commerce software provider reached US$13.3-billion. This surge in wealth is attributed to Shopify's stock crossing the $2,000 mark on the Toronto Stock Exchange and increasing optimism surrounding the company's second-quarter earnings. Analysts have raised their price targets for Shopify, anticipating its revenue to surpass US$1-billion for the first time in the June 30 quarter.

feed.title: Unboxing the WD_BLACK P10 Game Drive Gaming Hard Drive!
feed.summary: This article unboxes and reviews the WD_BLACK P10 Game Drive, a compact gaming HDD. The reviewer highlights its portability, cool design, and compatibility with gaming consoles like PlayStation 4 and Xbox One. It emphasizes the drive's utility for gamers running out of storage space for games, screenshots, and videos.

feed.title: Intel to build Qualcomm chips, aims to catch foundry rivals by 2025
feed.summary: Intel announced its factories will begin building chips for Qualcomm and Amazon, aiming to regain its leadership in chip manufacturing by 2025 against rivals like TSMC and Samsung. The company outlined a new roadmap with five chipmaking technologies and a revised naming scheme to reflect its competitive standing. This move signifies Intel's commitment to overcoming past delays and establishing itself as a leading foundry.

feed.title: Sparkline Launches Intangible Value ETF, ‘ITAN’
feed.summary: Sparkline Capital has launched the Sparkline Intangible Value ETF (ITAN), an actively managed fund that evaluates companies based on their "intangible value." This value is derived from four pillars: intellectual property, brand equity, human capital, and network effects, integrated with tangible assets using machine learning. The fund aims to provide a modern interpretation of value investing by focusing on high-quality companies with strong intangible drivers of growth.

feed.title: Amazon denies accepting Bitcoin, sends it tumbling
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc denied a media report suggesting it would accept Bitcoin payments by year-end, causing the cryptocurrency to tumble below $37,000. Earlier speculation, fueled by a job posting for a digital currency lead, had driven Bitcoin past $40,000. The company stated that while interested in the space, the specific plans for cryptocurrencies were not true.

feed.title: Workday (WDAY) Stock Falls After Amazon (AMZN) Ends Deal for HR Software
feed.summary: Workday Inc. shares experienced their largest drop in eight months after Amazon.com Inc. discontinued its use of Workday's human resources software. Business Insider reported the news, leading to a significant intraday share decline of up to 7.8%. Workday confirmed that the decision was made 18 months prior, citing Amazon's "unique set of needs."

feed.title: Stores may soon use one-third of their space for e-commerce fulfillment: report
feed.summary: A new report by Edge by Ascential suggests that stores worldwide could soon dedicate up to one-third of their physical space to e-commerce fulfillment. This shift is driven by the accelerated growth of online retail, which the firm estimates will account for 34.8% of global chain retail by 2023 and nearly 40% by 2025. While e-commerce growth has tempered in the U.S. post-lockdown, the pandemic significantly sped up digital disruption, making physical stores increasingly vital for both fulfillment and customer experience in an interconnected retail ecosystem.

feed.title: HP To Acquire Teradici: ‘Huge Asset’ In Remote Computing Push
feed.summary: HP Inc. announced its agreement to acquire Teradici, a high-performance remote computing software firm, to bolster its offerings in virtual computing technologies for the hybrid work market. Teradici's technology enables secure and high-fidelity remote access to computing, including for graphics-intensive workloads, and is platform-agnostic. This acquisition aims to complement HP's PC business with software and services, integrating well with solutions like Microsoft's Windows 365, and expanding opportunities for HP's channel partners.

feed.title: Anthem, Humana join hedge fund administrator to launch technology for PBMs
feed.summary: Anthem and Humana have formed a joint venture called DomaniRx with hedge fund administrator SS&C Technologies Holdings to develop a cloud-native claims adjudication platform for pharmacy benefit managers. SS&C Technologies will hold the majority stake, while Anthem and Humana will each hold a minority interest, investing approximately $925 million collectively. The platform aims to disrupt the industry with open-source technology, providing insights from millions of claims transactions and enabling customization for clients like Humana, its first customer.

feed.title: Exclusive: Jakks Pacific Unveils 2021 A Christmas Story, Super Mario Advent Calendars
feed.summary: Jakks Pacific is expanding its seasonal offerings with two new advent calendars for 2021, exclusively revealed by The Toy Book. One calendar is based on "A Christmas Story," featuring figurines and artifacts from the film in a pop-up diorama of the Christmas Story House. The other is a Nintendo Super Mario advent calendar, an Amazon exclusive, that includes 24 characters and accessories, with special Santa Mario and Snowman Mario figures.

feed.title: Microsoft acquires Seattle startup Suplari, which uses AI to analyze corporate spending
feed.summary: Microsoft has acquired Suplari, a Seattle-based startup that leverages artificial intelligence to help companies analyze and manage corporate spending effectively. Suplari's technology provides recommendations for cost savings and risk exposure by analyzing procurement and spending data, which Microsoft plans to integrate with its Dynamics 365 offering. This acquisition highlights the growing trend of using AI and machine learning to automate data-intensive financial processes.

feed.title: Walmart to sell its e-commerce technologies to other retailers
feed.summary: Walmart is making its e-commerce and retail technologies, previously used to transform its own business, available to other retailers through a strategic partnership with Adobe. This collaboration will integrate Walmart's Marketplace, fulfillment, and pickup technologies into the Adobe Commerce Platform, allowing small to mid-sized retailers to utilize Walmart's tools. The initiative aims to provide new revenue streams for Walmart while potentially boosting its Marketplace through increased product listings and offering an expanded set of customers to Adobe's retail clients.

feed.title: To Attract Gen Z Shoppers, Macy's Expands its Private Labels
feed.summary: Macy's has launched a new private label apparel line called "And Now This" specifically targeting Gen Z shoppers. This move is part of a broader trend among retailers to attract younger demographics with store-owned brands. The new line emphasizes a minimalist aesthetic, sharp price points, and a high concentration of denim, aiming to refresh Macy's appeal.

feed.title: Why Sonos and The North Face are putting an Antarctic expedition in your ears
feed.summary: Sonos and The North Face have partnered to launch "Never Stop Exploring," a new Sonos Radio channel featuring audio stories and field recordings from the adventures of North Face-sponsored athletes. This collaboration aims to showcase the portability of Sonos's Roam speaker and inspire listeners to connect with the outdoors through immersive audio experiences. The project involved composer Mikael Jorgensen translating expeditions into sonic journeys, offering a unique evolution in branded audio content.

feed.title: Traeger, Weber battle it out in barbecue IPO mini-boom as consumers spend billions on outdoor cooking
feed.summary: The pandemic-driven surge in at-home grilling has led to a mini-boom in barbecue company IPOs, with Traeger and Weber leading the charge. Traeger priced its IPO at $18, valuing it at $2 billion, while rival Weber is also planning to go public with an expected valuation between $4 billion and $6 billion. Other companies like BBQGuys are also entering the public market, capitalizing on consumers spending billions on outdoor cooking equipment.

feed.title: Wegmans announces second NYC location to open in 2023
feed.summary: Wegmans, the popular upstate grocery chain, has confirmed it will open its first Manhattan location in the former Kmart on Astor Place in the second half of 2023. This will be their second New York City store, following the successful opening of their Brooklyn Navy Yard location in 2019. The new 82,000-square-foot store will be located at 770 Broadway.

feed.title: AMZN Earnings: All you need to know about Amazon.com Q2 2021 earnings results
feed.summary: Amazon.com (NASDAQ: AMZN) reported its Q2 2021 earnings, with net sales increasing 27% year-over-year to $113.1 billion and net income reaching $7.8 billion, or $15.12 per share. Despite beating earnings estimates, revenue fell short of expectations, and the company's Q3 2021 sales outlook of $106-112 billion (10-16% growth) also disappointed analysts, leading to a 5% drop in shares during aftermarket trade.

feed.title: Kroger Unveils the Future of Grocery in Florida
feed.summary: Kroger has opened a new robotic fulfillment center in Groveland, Florida, marking its significant expansion into the Florida market without traditional stores. This 375,000-sq.-ft. facility, operational since March, is exceeding expectations and is part of Kroger's strategy to double its online business by 2023. The center utilizes advanced technology from Ocado and serves as a hub in a spoke model aimed at eventually covering all of South Florida.

feed.title: Netflix Transformers War for Cybertron Trilogy: Kingdom Series Available Now!
feed.summary: The final chapter of the Transformers: War for Cybertron Trilogy, titled "Kingdom," is now available to stream on Netflix. This series features the classic Beast Wars characters joining the conflict, with Autobots and Maximals teaming up against Decepticons and Predacons in a race to find the AllSpark. The Predacons hold a significant advantage through the mysterious Golden Disk.

feed.title: Pitney Bowes Names Nick Smith SVP Strategy, Product & Platform Management, Global Ecommerce; Taps Amazon Veteran Brent Cervenka to Lead Global Ecommerce Operations
feed.summary: Pitney Bowes Inc. (NYSE: PBI) has announced key leadership appointments in its Global Ecommerce division to strengthen its team ahead of the 2021 peak shipping season. Nick Smith has been named SVP Strategy, Product and Platform Management, Global Ecommerce, while Amazon veteran Brent Cervenka joins as SVP, Global Operations for Global Ecommerce. These appointments are part of a broader effort to recruit top talent and enhance the company's ecommerce logistics capabilities.

feed.title: Vittorio Cretella left school at 17 to join the Italian Army. Now he’s CIO at Procter & Gamble.
feed.summary: Vittorio Cretella, CIO of Procter & Gamble, discusses his unconventional career path from the Italian Army to leading digital transformation at the $300+ billion CPG company. He emphasizes the importance of diversity and inclusion in hiring, along with the pervasive role of digital transformation, machine learning, and cloud adoption in P&G's strategy. Cretella also highlights his work with NPower, a non-profit helping veterans and underserved youth find careers in tech, and P&G's neurodiversity hiring program.

feed.title: Google founders have sold $1 billion in stock since May, the most since 2017
feed.summary: Google co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin have sold over $1 billion worth of stock combined since May 2021, marking their first sales since 2017. Their stock sales, totaling more than $1.07 billion, occurred after the company's share price nearly tripled and Alphabet reported strong second-quarter earnings. Despite stepping down from executive roles in 2019, Page and Brin remain board members and control a majority of Alphabet's voting shares.

feed.title: 5 things to know before the stock market opens Friday
feed.summary: On Friday, July 30, the stock market was poised for a lower open, with Nasdaq futures leading declines due to Amazon's revenue miss. Despite this, all three major benchmarks were still on track for solid monthly gains. Investors were also digesting the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, which came in slightly below estimates, and mixed earnings reports from Dow companies like Chevron, Caterpillar, and Procter & Gamble.

feed.title: Klarna acquires platform that connects content creators, retailers
feed.summary: Klarna announced its acquisition of Apprl, a platform connecting content creators and retailers, to expand its retail marketing services. This move allows Klarna's partners to collaborate with creators on shoppable content and measure campaign results, aiming to enrich the shopping experience and accelerate retailer growth. The acquisition follows other strategic moves by Klarna, highlighting its entry into social commerce alongside other major companies.

feed.title: Netgear Wi-Fi 6 Orbi RBK353 review: high-speed Wi-Fi 6 mesh networking for less
feed.summary: The Netgear Wi-Fi 6 Orbi RBK353 offers an affordable mesh networking solution with Wi-Fi 6 support and good coverage, making it suitable for users who don't need advanced features. While it provides stable and fast Wi-Fi across a medium-sized home and includes Ethernet ports on all units, it lacks tri-band support and integrated parental controls, with extra security features costing more. Despite these minor drawbacks, it's recommended for those seeking the latest Wi-Fi standard at a reasonable price, though advanced users might prefer pricier Orbi models.

feed.title: How SAP and Deutsche Boerse Are Paving the Way to the Public Cloud for the Finance Industry
feed.summary: SAP and Deutsche Boerse have collaborated to create a framework for secure and compliant hosting of sensitive financial data in the public cloud, addressing the strict regulatory requirements of the financial sector. This solution, based on SAP Data Custodian, allows financial service providers like Deutsche Boerse to maintain full control over their data, ensure data sovereignty, and protect sensitive information while leveraging the scalability and agility of cloud technology. The initiative aims to enable more financial institutions to move critical operations to the cloud, overcoming concerns about security and compliance.

feed.title: PepsiCo
feed.summary: This article provides a corporate profile of PepsiCo, a global leader in the food and beverage industry. It highlights the company's history, leadership, global operations, commitment to sustainability, and extensive product range. The article also lists various related content covering PepsiCo's supply chain efficiency, sustainability efforts, and innovations within the industry.

feed.title: Reese Witherspoon’s Hello Sunshine to be be sold to media company backed by Blackstone
feed.summary: Reese Witherspoon's media company, Hello Sunshine, is being sold to a firm backed by Blackstone Group Inc. for approximately $900 million. This acquisition is part of a strategy to create an independent entertainment company for the streaming era, led by former Disney executives Kevin Mayer and Tom Staggs. Witherspoon will continue to operate Hello Sunshine and join the new company's board, aiming to further the mission of telling more women's stories.

feed.title: Google’s upcoming plans for Israel: a server farm and fiber optics network
feed.summary: Google is significantly expanding its operations in Israel following being awarded a government tender for cloud computing. This expansion includes establishing a server farm in the Sharon region and rolling out an ultra-high-speed fiber optics network, complementing its existing subsea cables. These investments aim to ensure maximum availability of Google's services, improve local internet infrastructure, and enhance Google's presence as a global telecommunications provider.

feed.title: Apple ranks as world's most profitable company: Report
feed.summary: Apple has been named the world's most profitable company for the 2020 fiscal year, according to the Fortune Global 500 list, with profits increasing 3.9% to $57.41 billion. This marks a shift from the previous two years where Saudi Aramco held the top spot. While Apple leads in profitability, Walmart remains the top company in terms of revenue on the global list.

feed.title: Why all roads of industry lead to purposeful innovation and a platform economy
feed.summary: The article discusses how the platform economy is transforming various industries, enabling faster innovation, increased collaboration, and better customer service. It highlights how companies adopting a platform mindset can overcome traditional business model inefficiencies and leverage interconnected tools, especially in sectors like AEC. The piece emphasizes the creation of value through compelling platform capabilities and the strategic decision of either building one's own platform or integrating with existing ones, citing examples like Tesla and Netflix.

feed.title: ¿Cuáles son las empresas españolas con más ingresos en 2020?
feed.summary: The article discusses the "Fortune Global 500" list for 2021, highlighting Spanish companies with the highest revenues in 2020. Banco Santander, Telefónica, and ACS lead among Spanish firms but have fallen in global rankings due to the pandemic's impact. Inditex and Naturgy Energy notably dropped off the list, while Walmart topped global rankings for the eighth consecutive year.

feed.title: Amazon to add delivery fee for Prime members who shop at Whole Foods
feed.summary: Amazon is introducing a $9.95 delivery fee for Prime members shopping at Whole Foods Markets in several U.S. cities, effective August 30. This move indicates challenges in grocery delivery economics, even for large retailers, and is described as a pilot program to cover operating costs rather than raising food prices. Grocery pickup will remain free for Prime members.

feed.title: DirecTV breaks free from AT&T
feed.summary: After a disastrous six-year ownership, AT&T has completed the spinoff of DirecTV, returning the satellite TV service to being a stand-alone company. AT&T acquired DirecTV for $49 billion but lost nearly 40% of its subscriber base, leading to high "churn" rates. The new DirecTV, with AT&T holding a 70% interest and private equity firm TPG owning 30%, aims to simplify its message and repair its reputation, focusing on content aggregation and distribution, though it plans to discontinue the NFL Sunday Ticket package after the 2022 season due to substantial losses.

feed.title: Gannett Relaunches ‘USA Today’ Streaming Video Platforms
feed.summary: Gannett is relaunching its USA Today streaming video platforms, including USA Today News and USA Today SportsWire, expanding their presence across more than a dozen FAST platforms like Xumo, Roku Channel, and Amazon Prime Video. This expansion has led to significant audience growth, reaching over 25 million monthly views, and a 200% increase in average view duration. Gannett is partnering with Amagi to facilitate this digital growth and enhance monetization opportunities for its premium sports and news content.

feed.title: AT&T's DirecTV to become standalone video business
feed.summary: AT&T Inc.'s satellite television provider, DirecTV, will become a standalone video business as part of a deal with buyout firm TPG Capital. The new entity will launch DirecTv Stream, a platform providing access to various streaming services. AT&T will retain a 70% ownership stake, while TPG Capital will own 30% of the new company, which was valued significantly lower than AT&T's initial purchase price.

feed.title: Deal Of The Century: How Michael Dell Turned His Declining PC Business Into A $40 Billion Windfall
feed.summary: Michael Dell orchestrated a massive comeback for his company, Dell Technologies, transforming it from a declining PC business into an IT infrastructure giant with a $40 billion windfall for investors and a personal net worth of $50 billion. Through strategic leveraged buyouts, including taking the company private in 2013 and acquiring EMC in 2016, Dell successfully pivoted towards data center infrastructure, cloud management services, and the "edge" market. His financial engineering acumen and willingness to take on debt, combined with a correct reading of technology industry trends, allowed him to reassemble his empire and spin off VMware, solidifying his position in enterprise technology.

feed.title: Baxter extends Amazon Web Services collaboration to support new digital health, data management projects
feed.summary: Baxter is expanding its long-term partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to focus on new digital health tools, supply chain efficiency, and deeper insights from business operations. This collaboration aims to enhance its DoseEdge software, improve supply chain responsiveness through AWS analytics, and further develop remote patient management systems like its AWS-powered Sharesource platform. The move is part of Baxter's ongoing "digital transformation" and builds on its recent sales growth.

feed.title: Pennsylvania Employers Must Pay for Time Spent in Security Screenings
feed.summary: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court ruled on July 21, 2021, that time employees spend waiting for and undergoing mandatory security screenings on an employer's premises is compensable "hours worked" under Pennsylvania law. This decision in In re Amazon.com, Inc. contrasts with the 2014 U.S. Supreme Court ruling in Integrity Staffing Solutions, Inc. v. Busk, which held such time is not compensable under the federal FLSA. Pennsylvania employers must now adjust their pay policies to comply, recognizing the state's stronger wage protections.

feed.title: Amazon and Best Buy Introduce New Insignia F50 Series Fire TVs
feed.summary: Amazon and Best Buy announced a new lineup of Best Buy Insignia F50 Series Fire TVs, marking an expansion of their three-year strategic collaboration. These new TVs feature enhanced 4K Ultra HD resolution with quantum dot technology, Dolby Vision HDR, DTS Virtual-X sound, and integrated Alexa voice controls for an immersive entertainment experience. The companies also highlighted the success of their in-store pick-up option for Fire TVs, allowing customers to order on Amazon.com and pick up at Best Buy stores.

feed.title: How to Use Snowflake to Connect Your Systems and Centralize Your Data to Prevent Customer Churn
feed.summary: This article explains how to use Snowflake as a data hub to centralize data from various systems, such as AS400 and Salesforce, to prevent customer churn. It details the steps for connecting AWS S3 data to Snowflake using Snowpipes and integrating Salesforce data, demonstrating how a Senior Data Architect would implement this solution. The integration aims to provide a holistic view of customer information, enabling proactive measures against churn.

feed.title: Baxter Begins Collaboration with Amazon Web Services
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. is accelerating its digital transformation by collaborating with Amazon Web Services (AWS) across digital health solutions, customer experience, and technology modernization. This multi-year strategic agreement focuses on leveraging cloud technology to personalize patient care, enhance customer experiences, and modernize business processes, building on their existing work of migrating Baxter's data centers to the cloud. The transformation has already enabled advancements in home dialysis patient management with Sharesource and pharmacy workflow with DoseEdge, while also improving supply chain resilience during the pandemic.

feed.title: The world’s richest company is not a tech firm says Fortune list
feed.summary: Fortune's annual Global 500 list for fiscal year 2020 revealed that Walmart is the world's richest company by revenue, not a tech firm, despite significant tech industry profits during the pandemic. China continues to dominate the list with 143 firms, while the US follows with 122. Brookfield Asset Management was the top Canadian firm, with Apple being the most profitable company overall.

feed.title: FedEx plans to hire 3,000 in Memphis, 80,000 nationwide
feed.summary: FedEx Corp. plans to hire 80,000 people nationwide, including 3,000 in Memphis, due to increased demand for residential delivery services driven by the e-commerce boom. The hiring initiative aims to address the rising need for its services across its various subsidiaries. This massive recruitment effort reflects the ongoing impact of e-commerce on the delivery industry.

feed.title: Coronavirus: Starbucks, McDonald’s, Home Depot to require workers wear masks inside all locations
feed.summary: Starbucks, McDonald’s, and Home Depot are reinstating indoor mask mandates for their employees due to the surge in COVID-19 infections caused by the delta variant. McDonald's and Target will specifically enforce these measures in high-risk transmission areas as identified by the CDC. These policy changes align with the CDC's updated recommendations urging vaccinated individuals to resume wearing masks indoors in certain settings.

feed.title: QQQ Versus QQQJ: Who’s Winning the Battle?
feed.summary: This article compares the performance of the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and the Invesco NASDAQ Next Gen 100 ETF (QQQJ). QQQ, which focuses on large-cap tech companies, has outperformed QQQJ, a mid-cap focused ETF, across various timeframes. Chad Morganlander of Washington Crossing Advisors favors QQQ due to its higher quality large-cap names and stronger balance sheets.

feed.title: Amazon invested nearly $15M in Seattle biotech company Nautilus, filing shows
feed.summary: Amazon has invested nearly $15 million in Nautilus Biotechnology, a Seattle-based biotech company co-founded in 2016 by Sujal Patel and Parag Mallick. This investment highlights Amazon's ongoing efforts to expand its presence in the healthcare sector. The disclosure comes from a recent filing.

feed.title: ETF of the Week: Bitwise Crypto Industry Innovators ETF (BITQ)
feed.summary: This article highlights the Bitwise Crypto Industry Innovators ETF (BITQ), which tracks companies generating most of their revenue from crypto business activities, as a top performer. The article discusses recent crypto market catalysts, including rumors of Amazon accepting crypto payments and statements from Elon Musk regarding Tesla's Bitcoin holdings and Jack Dorsey on Twitter's crypto integration. BITQ is presented as a way for investors to gain exposure to the crypto economy through "pure-play" companies like Coinbase, Silvergate Capital, and MicroStrategy, without directly owning cryptocurrencies.

feed.title: #DEFCON: A Bad eBook Can Take Over Your Kindle (or Worse)
feed.summary: A security researcher at Check Point Software Technologies demonstrated at DEF CON 29 how a malicious eBook could exploit Amazon Kindle devices, gaining root access and full control. The attack leverages two vulnerabilities (CVE-2021-30354 and CVE-2021-30355) that allow for remote code execution and local privilege escalation. This exploit could enable attackers to steal Amazon accounts, delete books, turn Kindles into bots, or brick the device, though Amazon has since fixed the reported issues.

feed.title: Goldman knows the way to a banker's heart is a free meal
feed.summary: Companies like Goldman Sachs, Havas SA, and Janus Henderson Group Plc are offering free meals and enhanced culinary experiences to entice employees back to the office as pandemic restrictions ease. They are using food perks to encourage regular attendance, manage work patterns, and reinvigorate office culture, hoping to fill expensive office spaces and foster collaboration. While some companies are investing heavily in food, others like Revolut, Microsoft, and Adobe are embracing more flexible or remote work models.

feed.title: ServiceNow Acquires Database Performance Software Company Swarm64 -
feed.summary: ServiceNow has acquired Swarm64, a Berlin-based database performance software company, to enhance its ability to scale digital workflows and handle growing data volumes for customers. Swarm64, founded in 2013 and previously venture-backed by Intel Capital and Xilinx, specializes in combining analytics and transactional database functionality. This acquisition aligns with ServiceNow's broader strategy of evolving its offerings beyond IT service management and expanding its partner ecosystem.

feed.title: Apple’s TV Box Is Now Mostly Pointless. Will That Change?
feed.summary: The article discusses how Apple's TV box, despite recent upgrades like a faster processor and new remote, has become mostly pointless in 2021.
