It questions whether the $199 price tag is justified given that content is now accessible on many devices, competing with cheaper alternatives like Amazon Fire and Google Chromecast. The author suggests that Apple needs to re-evaluate the device's purpose to remain relevant in the streaming market.

feed.title: Quantum Computing Stocks Are Having a Moment
feed.summary: This article explores the current state of quantum computing stocks, highlighting that while many large tech companies are "dabbling" in the field, pure-play quantum computing investments are still rare and often speculative. It reviews major players like IonQ, Microsoft, Amazon, Google, NVIDIA, Honeywell, and IBM, noting that many fundamental breakthroughs and commercially viable products are still years away. The author also warns against speculative investments, particularly those involving new SPACs and OTC stocks, and suggests that true quantum supremacy may not be publicly disclosed.

feed.title: Websites To Get Hired For Remote Virtual Assistant Jobs
feed.summary: This article lists several websites where individuals can find remote virtual assistant jobs, including Fiverr, Guru, Freelancer, UPWork, oDesk, and Amazon Mechanical Turk. It provides brief descriptions of how each platform works and encourages aspiring virtual assistants to build a strong online presence and portfolio to succeed in the competitive market. The author emphasizes professionalism, persistence, and continuous networking.

feed.title: Sephora’s beauty makeover at Kohl’s begins
feed.summary: Sephora is launching over 70 shop-in-shops within Kohl's stores this month, expanding its online presence and offering exclusive beauty brands like Fenty Beauty and Drunk Elephant. This initiative, announced in December, aims to replace Kohl's existing beauty departments, with plans for 200 shops by fall and 850 by 2023. This partnership follows Target's similar collaboration with Ulta Beauty, intensifying competition in the beauty retail sector.

feed.title: GE Healthcare Brings Radiology Without Walls to Resource-Constrained Small Imaging Centers With AI-Enabled, Cloud Imaging Solution
feed.summary: GE Healthcare has launched Edison True PACS, an AI-enabled, cloud-based Picture Archive and Communication System designed to help resource-constrained small imaging centers and community hospitals improve efficiency and diagnostic precision. This solution addresses challenges like radiologist shortages and IT resource limitations, offering remote reading capabilities and seamless integration of AI algorithms. It is available as a SaaS or on-premise offering with flexible subscription models to match varying organizational needs and budgets.

feed.title: Reese Witherspoon's $900-million deal
feed.summary: Reese Witherspoon recently sold a majority stake in her production company, Hello Sunshine, to a group backed by Blackstone, valuing the company at $900 million. This deal is part of a larger trend in Hollywood, driven by the streaming boom, where private equity firms are investing heavily in content creation and talent. The move allows creators like Witherspoon to maintain more control over their projects and maximize their value in a competitive market dominated by streaming giants.

feed.title: Amazon reveals massive insurance partnership
feed.summary: Marsh has partnered with Amazon to launch the Amazon Insurance Accelerator, a new digital insurance network designed to help small business sellers on Amazon secure affordable product liability coverage. This initiative comes as Amazon now requires third-party sellers to obtain product liability insurance after reaching $10,000 in sales in one month. The Accelerator provides US-based small business sellers with access to quotes and policies from several leading insurers, making it easier to meet Amazon's requirements and gain peace of mind.

feed.title: Workday selects Google Cloud as a preferred cloud partner
feed.summary: Workday Inc., a provider of financial and human capital management software, has chosen Google Cloud as a preferred cloud partner. This partnership will allow Workday customers in various industries to deploy Workday applications directly on Google's cloud infrastructure, giving them more deployment choices. The agreement follows recent reports of scalability issues for Workday's software with large customers like Amazon Web Services and the State of Maine.

feed.title: Oracle targets data warehouse rivals with automated features for HeatWave MySQL service
feed.summary: Oracle has enhanced its HeatWave MySQL service with new machine learning-based automation features, dubbed MySQL Autopilot, to improve performance and target rival analytical engines from Amazon, Google, and Snowflake. The company claims these features make queries significantly faster and more cost-effective. Additionally, Oracle introduced MySQL Scale-out Data Management and increased HeatWave cluster size, aiming to monetize its widely used MySQL engine through a managed service offering.

feed.title: Sonos Move vs. Apple HomePod: Which should you buy?
feed.summary: This article compares the Sonos Move and Apple HomePod, helping consumers decide which smart speaker best fits their needs. It highlights the Sonos Move's flexibility, portability, and compatibility with multiple voice assistants and music services, contrasted with the HomePod's high-quality sound and deep integration with the Apple ecosystem, particularly Apple Music and Siri. The comparison delves into features like weather resistance, battery life, and integration with smart home platforms, concluding that the choice depends on user preference for portability, voice assistant, and commitment to the Apple ecosystem.

feed.title: Marsh Brokers New Amazon Insurance Network Offering Coverage to Online Sellers
feed.summary: Marsh has partnered with Amazon to launch the Amazon Insurance Accelerator, a digital insurance network designed to help small businesses selling on Amazon secure product liability coverage. This initiative comes as Amazon stiffens its requirements for third-party sellers to obtain product liability insurance after reaching $10,000 in monthly sales. The platform connects sellers with a panel of insurers and digital distribution providers, offering competitive quotes and policies to fulfill Amazon's new mandate.

feed.title: Online Shopping Just Got Cheesier: Cheez-It® Debuts First Ever Online Store With An Exclusive New Flavor Drop
feed.summary: Cheez-It has launched its first-ever online store, CheezItHQ.com, offering exclusive products, merchandise, and limited-edition flavors. To celebrate, the brand is releasing a Cheez-It Extra Toasty Superfan 4-Pack featuring three new "Extra Toasty" variations. The new direct-to-consumer platform aims to connect with fans and bring food innovations to market faster.

feed.title: Expedia Group pushes back its full return to the office to January 2022
feed.summary: Expedia Group has announced a delay in its full return to office plans, now targeting January 2022 for a hybrid model due to evolving COVID-19 circumstances. This decision follows similar moves by other major tech companies like Amazon, Microsoft, Google, and Apple. The company's U.S. offices are open to vaccinated employees and visitors in the interim, with a global evaluation for similar approaches.

feed.title: ICICI Bank has done well on asset quality and growth compared to peers: Bank of America
feed.summary: Bank of America has re-iterated a 'buy' rating on ICICI Bank with a target price of Rs 820 per share, citing the bank's strong performance in asset quality and growth compared to its peers. Despite a challenging quarter for the banking sector, ICICI Bank showed healthy operating performance, good growth, and controlled slippages, with Bank of America upgrading its ROEs and earnings expectations. The fund house believes larger banks like ICICI are better positioned due to superior book mix and provisions, expecting better performance in the coming quarters.

feed.title: Amazon to construct delivery station in Pinellas Park
feed.summary: Amazon announced plans to build a new 40,000-square-foot delivery station in Pinellas Park, Florida, which will serve as a last-mile distribution point. This facility is part of Amazon's broader expansion in Florida, including a large fulfillment center in Tallahassee and five additional delivery stations set to open by the end of 2022, collectively creating 2,000 jobs in the state. Pinellas Park's Mayor Sandra Bradbury welcomed the new business, highlighting its potential for job creation in the area.

feed.title: Albertsons Names Sharon McCollam New President and Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Albertsons Companies announced the appointment of Sharon McCollam as its new President and Chief Financial Officer, effective September 7, 2021. McCollam, widely recognized for her role in Best Buy's Renew Blue transformation, will succeed Bob Dimond, who is retiring. She will oversee finance, corporate strategy, IT, supply chain, and property development, leveraging her expertise in retail operations and digital growth to advance Albertsons' omnichannel and transformation strategies.
feed.source: Abasto, Food and Beverage Industry News
feed.source_domain: Abasto, Food and Beverage Industry News

feed.title: Antisemitism Watchdog Gives Corporate Giants Failing Grades in Handling of Antisemitism
feed.summary: Stop Antisemitism released a report grading major U.S. corporations on their handling of antisemitism, giving "F" grades to Google, Unilever, and Cigna for various shortcomings. The report criticized Google for not firing an employee with a problematic past statement and omitting antisemitism from diversity standards, Unilever for Ben & Jerry's stance on "Occupied Palestinian Territory" and lack of Jewish representation in diversity policies, and Cigna for similar omissions. Only L'Oreal and American Eagle Outfitters received "A" grades for proactive measures against antisemitism and fostering Jewish employee groups, respectively, highlighting a general failure of corporations to protect Jewish employees and address rising antisemitism.

feed.title: Gartner Magic Quadrant for CCaaS 2021
feed.summary: Gartner has released its 2021 Magic Quadrant report for Contact Center as a Service (CCaaS), consolidating its previous regional reports into a single global analysis. The report categorizes CCaaS vendors into Leaders, Challengers, Visionaries, and Niche Players based on their adherence to core capabilities like channel-agnostic customer service journeys and intelligent self-service. Key leaders identified are Genesys, NICE CXone, and Talkdesk, while the report also details the strengths and focuses of other market players.

feed.title: What's Changed: 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud Infrastructure and Platform Services
feed.summary: This article analyzes the 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Cloud Infrastructure and Platform Services (CIPS), highlighting key movements and strategies of the seven leading providers: Alibaba Cloud, Amazon Web Services, Google, IBM, Microsoft, Oracle, and Tencent Cloud. AWS maintains its dominant leadership, with Microsoft and Google also holding strong positions in the Leaders quadrant by focusing on operational databases, big data, and edge computing, respectively. The report details the evolving strategies of Visionary and Niche Players, including Alibaba's expansion beyond Asia, Oracle's commitment to its cloud infrastructure, IBM's restructuring to focus on hybrid/edge computing, and Tencent's investment in network capabilities for gaming.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble set to dethrone Amazon as world’s biggest advertiser
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble is projected to surpass Amazon as the world's largest advertiser. This report from Ad Age Datacenter indicates a significant shift in global advertising spending.

feed.title: Sonos ARC Review: An Upgradeable and Flexible Soundbar with Captivating Sound
feed.summary: The review introduces the Sonos ARC as a high-end, modular soundbar system that delivers an immersive audio experience. It highlights the ARC's upgradeable hardware, seamless setup (especially for Apple users), and captivating sound quality, particularly when paired with a Sonos Sub. While praising its performance and modularity, the review also notes minor drawbacks like a hidden sleep timer, lack of HDMI expansion, and absence of DTS support.

feed.title: Where Will Competition in Big Tech Lead Investors?
feed.summary: The article discusses the competitive landscape between big tech companies, specifically Amazon and Microsoft, in the cloud computing sector. This rivalry, exemplified by their legal battles over government contracts, creates trading opportunities for investors. The Direxion Daily Dow Jones Internet Bull 3X Shares (WEBL) is highlighted for those bullish on the sector, while the Direxion Daily Cloud Computing Bear 2X Shares (CLDL) is suggested for investors anticipating a downturn in cloud computing.

feed.title: Anexinet To Acquire Cisco Partner Light Networks For ‘Full Stack’ Data Center Offering
feed.summary: Anexinet is set to acquire Light Networks, a Cisco Premier partner, to bolster its data center services, particularly in unified communications and contact center as a service. This acquisition, backed by Mill Point Capital, is Anexinet's second in a year, aiming to expand its "full stack" data center offerings and geographic footprint, specifically focusing on the East Coast and potentially other cloud capabilities. The founders of Light Networks, headquartered in Atlanta, will continue to lead the business.

feed.title: Amazon Web Services in a new partnership with Wyndham Hotels
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has announced a new global partnership with Wyndham Hotels & Resorts, the world's largest hotel franchising company. This collaboration aims to enhance digital guest services across Wyndham's 20 hotel brands by migrating core reservations, property management systems, and data processing to the AWS Cloud. The move is expected to improve operational efficiency, lower costs by 45%, and enable personalized guest experiences through technologies like machine learning and a new mobile application.

feed.title: Kroger kicks off pickup partnership with Google
feed.summary: Kroger has partnered with Google to integrate pickup service functionality into Google Maps for 96 stores across Ohio, Kentucky, and Indiana, with plans for further expansion. This allows shoppers to manage their orders, receive reminders, and notify stores of their arrival directly through the app. The initiative aims to enhance convenience, attract new shoppers, and capitalize on the surge in online grocery demand driven by the pandemic and the spread of the delta variant.

feed.title: JanSport Sues Wholesaler Over Amazon-Listed Products
feed.summary: JanSport has filed a lawsuit against Bravo Wholesale, alleging the wholesaler sold "liquidated, used and often defective" JanSport backpacks on Amazon.com, misrepresenting them as new. The lawsuit details consumer complaints about the quality of the products and claims Bravo Wholesale used JanSport's trademarks without permission. While Amazon is not named in the suit, the case highlights ongoing issues with trademark infringement on third-party marketplace platforms.

feed.title: Oracle MySQL champion Nipun Agarwal explains the challenges of scaling up algorithms for data migrations
feed.summary: Oracle's Nipun Agarwal discusses the challenges and enhancements of MySQL HeatWave, particularly its ability to scale algorithms for data migrations. Oracle has seen significant migrations to HeatWave from other cloud vendors, including AWS Aurora and Amazon RDS, driven by customer requests for more automation and greater scale in data analytics, leading to significant performance improvements and cost reductions. The company has also published benchmark data and scripts for competitors and customers to verify HeatWave's superior performance.

feed.title: Board of Directors
feed.summary: This article lists the members of the Joby Aviation Board of Directors, detailing their professional backgrounds and expertise. Each board member's biography highlights their relevant experience, particularly in technology, aviation, finance, and large-scale operations, which are valuable to Joby Aviation's mission. The board includes founders, seasoned executives from major tech and aviation companies, and finance professionals instrumental in the company's growth.

feed.title: How Consumers Are Using Mass Arbitration to Fight Amazon, Intuit, and Other Corporate Giants
feed.summary: Mass arbitration is emerging as a critical strategy for consumers to challenge large corporations like Amazon and Intuit. This approach involves consumers collectively filing thousands of individual arbitration claims, which can overwhelm companies with fees and pressure them into settlements, effectively turning traditional arbitration clauses against the corporations that implemented them. While not a complete solution, it has proven successful in several cases, prompting some companies to reconsider mandatory arbitration policies and highlighting the need for broader policy reforms to protect consumer rights.

feed.title: Alibaba Company Profile
feed.summary: This article provides a company profile of Alibaba, detailing its business model, various e-commerce platforms, and other digital services. It discusses the impact of recent regulatory measures and fines from the Chinese government on Alibaba's stock performance, noting a bearish trend despite continued revenue growth. The article also highlights investor interest in Alibaba's cloud computing division as a key growth area for the company.

feed.title: Microsoft Partnership Puts the 'S' & 'G' Into the 'E' in ESG
feed.summary: Microsoft has partnered with Volt Energy to bring carbon-neutral electricity to underserved communities, addressing both environmental and social justice issues within the ESG framework. This initiative, while small in scale regarding initial power purchase, is seen as a significant step in pushing the boundaries of corporate environmental and social leadership. The article also highlights the Putnam Sustainable Leaders ETF (PLDR), which invests in companies like Microsoft that demonstrate proactive leadership in ESG issues and includes Microsoft as a top holding.

feed.title: Vita Coco parent enters new functional beverage segment with Pwr Lift protein water
feed.summary: Vita Coco's parent company, All Market, has launched Pwr Lift, a new line of protein-infused waters, expanding its presence in the functional beverage market. The drinks, available in four flavors on Amazon, contain 10 grams of whey protein, B vitamins, and electrolytes, and are marketed as post-workout recovery beverages. This move comes as All Market prepares for a potential IPO, aiming to compete in a growing but competitive functional beverage sector.

feed.title: Google infringed five Sonos patents
feed.summary: A U.S. trade judge has ruled that Google infringed five patents belonging to Sonos, related to smart speakers and associated technology. This preliminary decision by the U.S. International Trade Commission could potentially lead to a ban on imports of Google's smart speakers and Pixel phones. Sonos expressed satisfaction with the ruling, which they see as validation of their claims of patent infringement by Google.

feed.title: Equifax Brings Differentiated Data to AWS Data Exchange
feed.summary: Equifax is expanding its cloud strategy by offering various differentiated data assets, including consumer credit, commercial marketing, and economic data, through AWS Data Exchange. This collaboration aims to provide business customers with easier and more secure access to critical insights for improved decision-making and revenue generation. The initiative builds on Equifax's $1.5 billion investment in its cloud infrastructure, ensuring rigorous data security and compliance.

feed.title: Amazon’s Move Into Podcasts Was a Big Advertising Play All Along
feed.summary: Amazon's recent acquisitions in the podcast space, including the distribution rights for "SmartLess," are primarily driven by an advertising strategy rather than just content distribution. The company is spending over $20 million annually for exclusive early access to popular podcasts, aiming to leverage the unique podcast advertising model where shows and networks sell their own ads. This move positions Amazon to compete with market leaders like Facebook and Google in the lucrative online advertising market, using podcast ad sales as a key entry point.

feed.title: CIBC, Itaú Unibanco, National Australia Bank and NatWest Group launch Global Open Finance Challenge
feed.summary: CIBC, Itaú Unibanco, National Australia Bank, and NatWest Group have launched the Global Open Finance Challenge, a virtual event to foster innovation in the global open finance ecosystem. Collaborating with Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Oolys, the challenge invites innovators to develop customer solutions, focusing on digital services, convenient banking access, and sustainability. The event aims to transform the financial industry by promoting secure data sharing and customized product offerings, with winning teams receiving incubation opportunities and exposure to banking executives.

feed.title: Music distribution startup Distrokid hits unicorn status with Insight Partners funding
feed.summary: Distrokid, an online music distribution startup, has achieved unicorn status after receiving new funding from Insight Partners, valuing the company at $1.3 billion. Founded in 2003, Distrokid allows musicians to upload unlimited music to various online stores and streaming services for a flat annual fee, while retaining 100% of their earnings. The company claims to distribute over a third of all new music globally.

feed.title: Walmart Launches Logistical Revolution
feed.summary: Walmart is partnering with Symbotic, a robotics company, to automate its regional distribution network in 25 of its 42 centers. This initiative aims to revolutionize product distribution, cut costs, and increase efficiency in its supply chain operations. The move is a strategic step to compete with Amazon in the soaring e-commerce market, involving significant upfront investment intended to pay off through reduced labor costs and faster fulfillment.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble regains its title as top global advertiser
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble has reclaimed its position as the world's top advertiser, surpassing Amazon, with a marketing expenditure of $11.5 billion in its last fiscal year. This resurgence highlights the CPG giant's strategy of maintaining ad spending even during difficult economic times, a move that brand consultants argue has consistently benefited the company. While Amazon has the capacity to increase its ad spending significantly, P&G's continued investment in marketing has proven effective in sustaining its brand presence and sales growth.

feed.title: The Digital Pulse: Outbrain, GearBrain's Content Recommendation Engine of Choice
feed.summary: GearBrain introduces "The Digital Pulse," a new monthly feature focusing on digital trends, with its inaugural column highlighting Outbrain Inc. (OB). The article explains why GearBrain chose Outbrain as its content recommendation engine, citing better quality recommendations, higher revenue generation, and minimal impact on user experience and site speed compared to competitors like Taboola (TBLA). GearBrain emphasizes that revenue from Outbrain helps cover editorial costs while ensuring a positive user experience.

feed.title: Opinion: Bullish optimism boosting ‘unicorn’ stocks is more like delirium — and a harsh dose of reality is coming
feed.summary: The article argues that the current bullish optimism surrounding "unicorn" stocks is akin to delirium, suggesting that a harsh dose of reality is imminent for these highly valued private companies. It draws a parallel to the early, uncertain stages of now-successful tech giants, highlighting that most "half-baked ideas" fail, and entrepreneurs often operate without hard evidence. The piece implies that the market is overvaluing these companies and that their current high valuations are unsustainable.

feed.title: Wipro's former COO Bhanumurthy Ballapuram joins Google Cloud
feed.summary: Bhanumurthy Ballapuram, former President and COO of Wipro Ltd., has joined Google Cloud as Vice President for the Japan and Asia Pacific region. This appointment is part of Google Cloud's strategy to intensify its competition with rivals Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web Services in the cloud-computing market, amidst its ongoing expansion and executive hiring. Ballapuram will work with regional leadership to further Google Cloud's enterprise momentum and customer success.

feed.title: MicroSectors™ Launches FANG & Innovation ETNs, 'BULZ' & 'BERZ'
feed.summary: REX Shares, LLC announced the launch of two new MicroSectors Exchange Traded Notes (ETNs), BULZ and BERZ, issued by the Bank of Montreal. These ETNs are linked to the Solactive FANG Innovation Index, which tracks major U.S.-listed tech and internet/media companies. The products aim to offer sophisticated investors leveraged and inverse leveraged exposure to prominent technology and innovation stocks.

feed.title: Digital Learning Platforms Market to Eyewitness Massive Growth by 2026 | Canvas, Nearpod, Top Hat
feed.summary: A new research study by HTF MI evaluates the Worldwide Digital Learning Platforms Market, forecasting massive growth by 2026. The report assesses market risks, highlights opportunities, and provides strategic decision-making support, covering key players like Canvas, Khan Academy, Nearpod, and Top Hat. It details market trends, growth drivers, technologies, and investment structures, segmenting the market by type (Web-based, On-Premises, Mobile) and application (Corporate, K-12, Higher Education) across various global regions.

feed.title: Amazon Hits Chinese Sellers With Crackdown on Fake Reviews
feed.summary: Amazon has begun cracking down on Chinese sellers who use "pay-for-praise" schemes and other policy violations to manipulate product reviews. This shift in enforcement has led to the suspension of over 50,000 Chinese retail accounts, causing an estimated $15.4 billion in lost sales, as Amazon seeks to restore integrity to its marketplace. Previously, the company had been more lenient to encourage low-priced goods from Chinese merchants, despite rules against incentivized reviews.

feed.title: Amazon Enters India Wealth Management with Investment in Smallcase Technologies
feed.summary: Amazon has made its first investment in India's wealth management sector by participating in a $40 million funding round for fintech startup Smallcase Technologies Pvt. Smallcase, founded in 2015, offers weighted portfolios of stocks and ETFs and has seen its user base double to over 3 million amidst a retail investor boom in India. This investment marks Amazon's expansion into wealth management, following previous fintech investments in India in insurance and credit.

feed.title: GDS Q2 results: Company acquires data center campus in Beijing, developing in Macau
feed.summary: GDS Holdings reported increased revenues and losses in Q2, with revenues up 38.9 percent to RMB1.86 billion ($288.7 million) and net loss at RMB298.5 million ($46.2 million). The Chinese data center company expanded significantly, bringing six new data centers online, starting construction on five more, and acquiring facilities in Beijing and Shenzhen. GDS is also extending its reach into Hong Kong and Macau, with a new 20MW data center planned for Macau and a 7,200 sqm facility in Hong Kong.

feed.title: David Droga, CEO and Creative Chairman, Accenture Interactive
feed.summary: Accenture has appointed David Droga, founder of Droga5, as the new CEO and creative chairman of Accenture Interactive, effective September 1, 2021. He replaces Brian Whipple, who is retiring after leading Accenture Interactive to significant growth, including $10.6 billion in revenue in fiscal year 2020. Droga will focus on driving creative excellence, customer experience, and business innovation, leveraging Accenture's capabilities to sustain business growth.

feed.title: Is Clean Energy Fuels Stock A Buy After Declining 8% Last Week?
feed.summary: Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE) saw its stock decline by 8% over the last week and 5% over the past month following mixed Q2 2021 earnings. Despite this, the company, which focuses on renewable natural gas (RNG) from animal waste, is considered a potential buy for long-term investors due to increasing urgency in climate change efforts, favorable regulations, and deals with major energy companies and Amazon. The company is actively investing in increasing its RNG supply and aims for 100% RNG sales at its stations within five years, positioning it well in the growing clean energy market.

feed.title: Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless Review: Upper-middle Class
feed.summary: The Corsair HS80 RGB Wireless headset offers impressive sound quality with Dolby Atmos, a clear microphone, and PS5 compatibility, making it a strong contender in the mid-range gaming headset market. Despite requiring two software applications and having limited size adjustments, its solid build and premium feel justify its price. The headset is ideal for PC and PS5 gamers seeking excellent audio performance and communication.

feed.title: Rapid, Automated Firewall Deployment Comes to AWS
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has extended its VM-Series Next-Generation Firewall (NGFW) Orchestration feature to Amazon Web Services (AWS), simplifying the deployment, management, scaling, and monitoring of virtual firewalls. This functionality, previously announced for Microsoft Azure, aims to save time and effort for enterprises securing their AWS environments. It automates operational tasks and configures necessary AWS resources, providing faster security deployment, reduced complexity, and enhanced scalability for network security teams.

feed.title: For Long-Term Investors, a QQQ-to-QQQM Switch May Make Sense
feed.summary: The article discusses how long-term investors might benefit from switching from the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) to the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) due to QQQM's lower expense ratio. While QQQM is newer and less liquid, its cost savings over time make it an attractive option for buy-and-hold investors seeking exposure to the Nasdaq-100 Index. The Nasdaq-100 Index has consistently outperformed other benchmarks, making both ETFs appealing for growth-oriented portfolios.

feed.title: Big retailers book pricey private cargo ships in holiday scramble
feed.summary: Major retailers like Walmart, Home Depot, and Ikea are chartering private cargo ships and buying their own containers to bypass supply chain disruptions ahead of the holiday season. These unprecedented and costly measures are being taken to ensure shelves remain stocked amid surging consumer demand and port congestion. This strategy gives larger retailers a competitive advantage over smaller businesses facing inventory shortages.

feed.title: Big Tech Is in a Perilous Moment. The Stocks Are Unstoppable.
authors: Eric J. Savitz and Max A. Cherney
feed.summary: Despite significant regulatory scrutiny, major tech companies like Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Facebook have demonstrated unstoppable stock performance. During the April to June quarter, these five giants collectively generated $332 billion in revenue, a 36% increase year over year, with all reporting better-than-expected profits. Interestingly, most of their stocks sold off slightly after the positive earnings announcements.

feed.title: Toys R Us shops are coming to hundreds of Macy's stores
feed.summary: Toys R Us is set to open shops in hundreds of Macy's stores, marking a significant return for the toy retailer. This partnership aims to bring the "world's biggest toy store" experience back to consumers through Macy's retail footprint. The move follows previous efforts to revitalize the Toys R Us brand after its bankruptcy.

feed.title: With Amazon MemoryDB, AWS takes aim at Redis in the cloud
feed.summary: AWS has launched Amazon MemoryDB for Redis, a new managed Redis-compatible in-memory database service. This service aims to compete with existing offerings from Redis, Google, and Microsoft by providing high performance and low latency for Redis users within the AWS cloud. While Redis acknowledges the competition, it emphasizes its differentiated enterprise functionality and multi-cloud deployment options.

feed.title: Cybersecurity Experts Worried by Chinese Firm’s Control of Smart Devices
feed.summary: Cybersecurity experts are concerned about Chinese firm Tuya Inc.'s control over smart devices given China's law requiring companies to share data with the government. This raises significant privacy and security issues for consumer data collected through IoT devices in U.S. households and other sectors. Experts are urging U.S. lawmakers to implement regulations or bans on Tuya and other Chinese tech companies to mitigate these potential national security and privacy risks.
feed.source: VOA - Voice of America English News
feed.source_domain: VOA - Voice of America English News

feed.title: Amazon vs. Netflix: Which is a better growth stock to buy?
feed.summary: This article analyzes Amazon (AMZN) and Netflix (NFLX) as growth stocks, comparing their financial performance and future prospects. While both companies show strong growth, the author suggests Amazon might be the better long-term investment due to its diversified business, sustainable growth, and strong competitive advantages in e-commerce and cloud computing. Netflix, despite its rapid earnings growth, has a higher price-to-earnings ratio and a more specialized business model.

feed.title: Intel wins Defense Department contract for cutting-edge chip foundry services
feed.summary: Intel has secured a contract with the U.S. Department of Defense to provide chip manufacturing services through its new Foundry Services division. This initiative, part of the RAMP-C program, aims to expand domestic semiconductor production for defense applications. Intel will collaborate with partners like Cadence Design Systems and Synopsys to develop and test semiconductors using its advanced Intel 18A manufacturing process, expected to be operational by early 2025.

feed.title: STARTUP STAGE: StayTick is a membership-based hotel booking platform
feed.summary: StayTick is a membership-based hotel booking platform that offers users savings of up to 25% on hotel bookings for an annual fee of €25. The company uses its proprietary technology to find and compare hotel rates, highlighting the savings for its members. StayTick aims to expand its market presence in Europe and potentially add other travel products, while relying on direct outreach and partnerships rather than competing with large OTAs on marketing spend.

feed.title: Official Disney Castle Posted on Zillow For Only $157.99
feed.summary: Hasbro has launched a unique marketing campaign for its new Disney Princess Ultimate Celebration Castle by listing it on Zillow as an official home. This initiative, part of Disney's Ultimate Princess Celebration and World Princess Week, allows "buyers" to browse an interactive listing complete with intricate details, room descriptions, and fictional previous visitors. The toy, standing four feet tall, is available for purchase on Amazon for $157.99, blending real estate fantasy with product promotion.

feed.title: AWS, Azure, Google Cloud: Which Will Pay You the Most?
feed.summary: A Stack Overflow Developer Survey reveals that among major cloud platforms, Amazon Web Services (AWS) specialists earn the highest median salaries, followed by Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud. The article highlights that professionals focusing on IBM or Oracle clouds might be at a disadvantage regarding pay, likely due to lower demand for these platforms. It emphasizes the importance of continuous learning and skill development in the evolving cloud computing landscape.

feed.title: He built an $8.9 billion fortune. Then the controversies began.
feed.summary: Bom Kim, founder of South Korean e-commerce giant Coupang, amassed an $8.9 billion fortune and was initially seen as a new kind of self-made billionaire. However, months after Coupang's blockbuster IPO, a series of controversies have emerged, causing some South Koreans to re-evaluate their perception of Kim and his company. The article highlights the shift from initial praise for his wealth generation to current scrutiny over business practices.

feed.title: Better operational decision-making through machine learning
feed.summary: TC Energy, a leader in the energy industry, is leveraging machine learning to enhance operational decision-making, particularly for its Gas Controllers. Through a collaboration with Amazon Web Services, they developed "Autonomous Pipeline," a system that analyzes vast data to provide real-time operational recommendations, mimicking experienced leadership. This innovation aims to improve safety, cost-efficiency, create market opportunities, and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.

feed.title: Like the QQQ ETF? Don't Forget About 'QQQM'
feed.summary: This article highlights the Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM) as an alternative to the popular Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) for long-term investors. While QQQ is favored by traders, QQQM offers a lower expense ratio and a more accessible share price, making it suitable for buy-and-hold strategies aiming to capitalize on the growth of Nasdaq's largest non-financial companies.

feed.title: White House to tackle cyber challenges with Apple, IBM, insurance CEOs
feed.summary: The White House is hosting a cybersecurity event bringing together top executives from major tech companies like Apple, IBM, Microsoft, and Google, alongside financial and energy sector leaders and the CEO of Travelers Companies Inc. The discussion will focus on ransomware, critical infrastructure, supply chain security, cybersecurity education, and data breach insurance, aiming to secure public commitments for improved IT security measures and workforce training. The event highlights the unique inclusion of the insurance industry, whose policies are seen as crucial for widespread improvements in cyber defense.

feed.title: Urban Outfitters’ Nuuly Is Launching A Thrift Store App
feed.summary: Urban Outfitters' parent company, URBN, is launching Nuuly Thrift, a new app for online resale. This platform will be a sister to their existing rental service, Nuuly, and will allow users to buy and sell apparel and accessories from various brands using "Nuuly Cash" credits. The move aims to capitalize on the rapidly growing online resale market, which is projected to be worth over $77 billion in five years.

feed.title: South Korea set to curb Google, Apple commission dominance
feed.summary: South Korea is poised to pass legislation, dubbed the "Anti-Google law," that would prevent Google and Apple from mandating their proprietary payment systems for in-app purchases, effectively stopping them from levying commissions. This move would make South Korea the first major economy to impose such restrictions on the tech giants, who currently charge up to 30% commission. The legislation has drawn criticism from Apple and industry groups representing U.S. tech companies, who argue it could risk users' privacy, reduce trust in app store purchases, and potentially violate trade commitments.

feed.title: Global X S&P 500 Tail Risk ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Global X S&P 500 Tail Risk ETF (XTR), which was established on August 25, 2021, by Global X. The ETF, with $3.96 million in AUM and 504 holdings, focuses on large-cap equities and aims to protect against significant market downtrends by purchasing quarterly Index put options. The primary holdings include major tech companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet.

feed.title: SK Telecom gets into subscription business
feed.summary: SK Telecom is launching a new subscription service called "T Universe" on August 31st, offering discounts and benefits at various brands including Amazon and 11st. The service, with two plans under 10,000 won, aims to capitalize on the growing subscription market and attract millennial and Gen Z consumers. SK Telecom anticipates initial losses but expects to expand its user base to 36 million and achieve 8 trillion won in gross merchandise volume by 2025.

feed.title: Target's stock buybacks represent a bet it will be more valuable in the future
feed.summary: Target's recent authorization of a $15 billion stock repurchase program is examined in the context of capital allocation. The article argues that while buybacks are often seen as short-term thinking, they can be a disciplined use of capital when other investment opportunities don't meet ROI criteria. This strategy reflects a long-term belief in the company's future value, as demonstrated by past buybacks at significantly lower prices.

feed.title: Amazon Announces Two New Cybersecurity Initiatives Aimed To Protect Organizations and Individuals
feed.summary: Amazon announced two new cybersecurity initiatives to protect organizations and individuals: making its Security Awareness training available to the public and offering free Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) devices to qualified AWS customers. The Security Awareness training aims to educate against cyberattacks like social engineering, while MFA devices provide a stronger security posture for sensitive information. These initiatives are designed to help combat increasing cybersecurity threats by enhancing education and providing stronger security tools.

feed.title: Six network protocols that signal an incoming DDoS attack
feed.summary: DDoS attacks, though sometimes overshadowed by ransomware, have seen a significant increase in volume and frequency. Security and network operations teams can detect impending DDoS attacks by monitoring specific network traffic types. The article outlines six common network protocols (UDP, CLDAP, DNS, TCP-SYN, Application Flooding, ICMP) often abused in DDoS attacks and offers insights into the warning signs to watch for, emphasizing the importance of network visibility, packet brokers, and packet capture systems for detection and mitigation.

feed.title: Alphabet’s Wing Nears 100,000th Package Delivery
feed.summary: Alphabet Inc.'s Wing subsidiary is close to delivering its 100,000th package, demonstrating rapid growth in the drone-delivery sector. The company is bullish on the long-term efficiency and profitability of drone delivery, with plans to expand operations in the U.S. and Finland. Wing anticipates that this automated delivery method will eventually be more cost-effective than traditional ground delivery.

feed.title: Urban Outfitters to Exit China Market
feed.summary: Urban Outfitters is closing its Tmall store and exiting the China market, citing a "global strategy adjustment" and following other international brands that have struggled there. The move highlights the intense competition from local players and online sellers, despite China's robust apparel market. This marks the brand's complete departure as it has no physical presence or other e-commerce channels in China.

feed.title: Walmart Goes Up Against Amazon and Uber to Handle Your Last-Mile Deliveries
feed.summary: Walmart has launched Walmart GoLocal, a last-mile delivery service leveraging its Spark platform to deliver goods from third-party merchants. This move positions Walmart in direct competition with major players like Amazon, Uber, and Instacart in the e-commerce delivery market. While it aims to create new revenue streams and utilize existing logistics efficiently, the service faces potential challenges including a nationwide shortage of delivery drivers.

feed.title: Microsoft hires former Amazon cloud executive Charlie Bell
feed.summary: Microsoft has hired Charlie Bell, a prominent former Amazon cloud executive, as a corporate vice president. Bell's move to Microsoft's Azure cloud business is seen as a strategic win, as Azure competes directly with Amazon Web Services (AWS), where Bell previously held a top leadership role for 15 years. His initial role at Microsoft is currently listed under the human resources department, which is considered unusual given his background.

feed.title: SK Telecom Introduces New Subscription Service
feed.summary: SK Telecom launched T Universe, a new subscription platform, aiming for 36 million subscribers and 8 trillion won in annual gross merchandise value by 2025. This service partners with major companies like Amazon, E-Mart, Starbucks, Google One, and Xbox Game Pass to offer a wide array of products and digital services. The company plans to leverage AI and a network of offline stores to provide personalized subscription offerings, anticipating significant growth in the global subscription market.

feed.title: Walmart's media arm to offer brands a tool to help manage digital ads
feed.summary: Walmart's advertising division, Walmart Connect, is launching a demand-side platform (DSP) to help brands more efficiently manage and purchase digital ad space. This new tool, developed in partnership with The Trade Desk, will leverage Walmart's extensive shopper data to improve ad targeting and measure sales impact both online and in-store. The platform aims to expand Walmart's advertising revenue, competing with Amazon and other retailers.

feed.title: Global X NASDAQ 100 Tail Risk ETF Holdings
feed.summary: This article details the holdings of the Global X NASDAQ 100 Tail Risk ETF (QTR), which was established on August 25, 2021, and focuses on large-cap equities. The ETF aims to mitigate "tail risk" by investing in NASDAQ 100 stocks and purchasing quarterly index put options. It lists the top holdings including NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, along with recent changes in its portfolio.

feed.title: Jessica Alba Shares Her Back to School Must-Haves
feed.summary: Jessica Alba, founder of The Honest Company, shared her back-to-school essentials for parents and students, focusing on clean and sustainable products. Her recommendations include Back to School Kits for classrooms with effective yet gentle cleaning supplies, reusable face masks, sanitizers, and wipes. Alba also highlighted self-care items like soaking salts and a calming bath set to help unwind during the stressful school year.

feed.title: As It Faces More Scrutiny, Apple Will Reduce App Store Fees If Publishers Provide Apple News Content
feed.summary: Apple announced a new News Partner Program that will reduce its App Store commission from 30% to 15% for news publishers who provide content to Apple News. This initiative comes as Apple faces increasing scrutiny and criticism regarding its App Store fees from developers, competitors, and regulators. To qualify, publishers must supply all their content in Apple's preferred format, provide metadata, and maintain an active presence in markets where Apple News is available.

feed.title: Who will dominate the tech arms race?
feed.summary: The article explores the competitive landscape of quantum computing, focusing on the race between tech giants like Google and

feed.title: Electric vehicle company Rivian has confidentially filed for an IPO
feed.summary: Electric vehicle startup Rivian has confidentially filed paperwork with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission to go public. The company, backed by investors like Ford and Amazon, has raised approximately $10.5 billion to date and was valued at $27.6 billion in January. Rivian plans to deliver its first R1T pickup truck in September.

feed.title: Gap Inc. acquires 3D fitting room startup Drapr
feed.summary: Gap Inc. has acquired Drapr, a 3D virtual try-on technology startup, to enhance the online fitting experience for its customers and drive its digital transformation. This acquisition follows similar moves by other retailers like Walmart and Snapchat to integrate fit tech. Gap hopes Drapr's technology will help solve fit issues, reduce returns for its brands, and complements other strategic initiatives like its investment in Obé fitness and Old Navy's Bodequality size-inclusive program.

feed.title: Amazon acquisitions: What does Amazon own?
feed.summary: This article details Amazon's extensive acquisition history, highlighting how the company expanded from an online bookstore to one of the world's most valuable companies by acquiring over 100 businesses across various sectors. It lists Amazon's largest acquisitions, such as Whole Foods and MGM, and also touches upon Jeff Bezos's other ventures outside of Amazon, like The Washington Post and Blue Origin. The article emphasizes Amazon's "need-to-win mentality" in driving its acquisition strategy.

feed.title: World's Biggest Stock Indices | Index Fundamentals
feed.summary: This article explains the key factors that influence the prices of major global stock indices, including the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average, FTSE 100, and DAX. It highlights how economic growth, dominant sectors, index weighting methodologies, and currency strength play significant roles in their performance. The piece offers a foundational understanding for traders looking to grasp index movements.

feed.title: XPO to stop hauling inbound LTL traffic in Albany, NY
feed.summary: XPO Logistics Inc. will cease accepting inbound LTL shipments destined for Amazon warehouses in Albany, N.Y., starting Monday. This decision, revealed through a memo from XPO’s national 3PL account executive, is seen as a reaction to Amazon-bound freight monopolizing XPO's trailer capacity and causing delays. The ban aims to clarify the extent of its impact on third-party logistics providers (3PLs) and potentially address diluted margins from such traffic.

feed.title: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts Taps AWS to Upgrade Global Cloud Infrastructure
feed.summary: Wyndham Hotels & Resorts has partnered with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to enhance its global cloud infrastructure and introduce new guest services across its 21 hotel brands. This collaboration is a key part of Wyndham's multi-year digital transformation, aiming to automate reservations, simplify operations for franchisees, and improve the guest experience in over 90 countries. The move will allow Wyndham to increase business performance, reinvest 45% of reduced data center costs, and achieve its goal of running 90% of its infrastructure in the cloud.

feed.title: This Toy From The Mid 2000’s Is Now Worth Thousands
feed.summary: The article discusses the resurgence in value of the discontinued miniature wargaming system, Heroscape, from the mid-2000s. Originally costing tens of dollars, master sets and waves are now selling for hundreds to thousands of dollars, even up to $4,500 for a complete set. The interlocking hexagonal tiles and mint-condition figures with cards are particularly valuable, with even damaged parts seeing significant price increases.

feed.title: Kroger is delivering groceries around Florida now. What to know and should Publix worry?
feed.summary: Kroger has launched an e-commerce grocery delivery service in Florida using an e-commerce hub in Groveland, covering a 90-mile radius. This marks Kroger's first e-commerce venture in Florida without brick-and-mortar stores, utilizing automated warehouses and Kroger-trained employees for delivery. The move is expected to introduce competition to the Florida grocery market, particularly for established retailers like Publix.

feed.title: BORROWING TO UPGRADE: Healthcare development projects to be funded by loan from Spanish bank
feed.summary: The Bahamian government plans to invest $100 million over the next 18 months to significantly upgrade the country's health infrastructure, addressing issues like limited bed space and insufficient equipment. This investment is being funded by a MIGA-guaranteed loan from Banco Santander SA of Spain, with $89 million specifically for modernizing the public health system in New Providence to respond to COVID-19 and build post-pandemic resilience. Additionally, negotiations are underway for an estimated $40 million MIGA-guaranteed loan from the same bank to construct a climate-resilient facility at Rand Memorial Hospital in Grand Bahama.

feed.title: Arlo Offers Comprehensive Third-Party Compatibility With Amazon, Apple, Google, Samsung, And Others For Seamless Smart Home Security Experience
feed.summary: Arlo Technologies announced enhanced compatibility for its smart home security systems with major platforms like Amazon Alexa, Apple HomeKit, Google Home Assistant, IFTTT, and Samsung SmartThings. These integrations provide users with seamless control over their Arlo devices through voice commands, notifications, and live video feeds on various smart home devices, reflecting Arlo's commitment to a robust and integrated security experience. The updates aim to offer peace of mind regardless of the user's preferred smart home ecosystem.

feed.title: Amazon Web Services works with Adobe Experience Cloud as it reimagines B2B marketing
feed.summary: This article details how Amazon Web Services (AWS) leveraged Adobe Experience Cloud to transform its B2B marketing strategy, particularly in response to the COVID-19 pandemic. It highlights AWS's successful pivot to virtual events, such as re:Invent, and how Adobe's analytics, personalization, and marketing automation tools enabled a seamless and personalized digital customer journey. The collaboration aims to further enhance personalization and optimize customer engagement in future hybrid events.

feed.title: JD.com ramps up omnichannel approach as it looks beyond e-commerce
feed.summary: JD.com is expanding its omnichannel fulfillment strategy by incorporating brick-and-mortar retailers and growing its warehouse footprint. This shift emphasizes supply chain services over pure e-commerce, with the company now identifying as a "supply chain-based technology and service provider." The goal is to leverage inventory from physical stores for faster deliveries and greater efficiency, aiming for long-term growth by enhancing logistics capabilities and customer experience.

feed.title: 13 Undervalued Wide-Moat Companies With Exceptional Leadership
feed.summary: This article identifies 13 undervalued wide-moat companies with exceptional leadership, as determined by Morningstar's Capital Allocation ratings. The companies listed earn an "Exemplary" capital allocation rating and are trading in the 4- and 5-star range. The article highlights Amazon, Compass Minerals, and Roche Holding ADR with detailed analyst commentary on their capital allocation strategies.

feed.title: HPE wins $2B contract with the NSA
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has secured a 10-year, $2 billion contract with the National Security Agency (NSA). This agreement involves the NSA leveraging HPE's high-performance computing technology through the HPE GreenLake platform. HPE will build and manage a comprehensive HPC solution, encompassing HPE Apollo systems and HPE ProLiant servers, capable of processing vast amounts of data and supporting AI capabilities, to be hosted in a QTS data center and operational by 2022.

feed.title: 7 Robotics Stocks to Buy for Elon Musk’s Big ‘Tesla Bot’ Bet
feed.summary: This article highlights seven robotics stocks that are poised for lucrative returns, driven by the increasing demand for robotics across various industries and tech giants like Tesla and Amazon. It discusses the market growth projections for industrial robotics and provides detailed analysis, financial data, and future outlook for each recommended stock, including ABB, AeroVironment, Global X Robotics & Artificial Intelligence ETF, Intuitive Surgical, iRobot, Oceaneering International, and Raytheon Technologies. The author emphasizes that these companies leverage automation and AI to enhance efficiency and address evolving market needs.

feed.title: Amazon reveals plans to hire 55,000 corporate and tech workers
feed.summary: Amazon plans to hire 55,000 new corporate and tech employees worldwide, with 40,000 in the U.S., focusing on e-commerce, cloud computing, and online advertising divisions. This hiring surge, announced ahead of its Career Day 2021, will also expand its satellite internet business and increase its corporate and tech workforce by 20%. The company continues to invest in new office spaces and logistics facilities to support its rapid growth.

feed.title: Discover More VM-Series Efficiency with Amazon Web Services VPC More Specific Routing
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks announced that its VM-Series virtual firewalls now integrate with the new Amazon Web Services VPC More Specific Routing feature. This integration enhances security efficiency by allowing seamless insertion of VM-Series firewalls to protect intra-VPC traffic and improves visibility for internet egress traffic from private subnets. This development makes it easier to deploy secure production-grade applications and strengthens overall security posture in AWS cloud environments.

feed.title: Focus: Amazon's new union battle: Teamsters go local to snarl expansion
feed.summary: The Teamsters Union is shifting its strategy to combat Amazon's expansion by focusing on local government challenges to derail new projects and calling for better labor standards. This approach follows an unsuccessful attempt by another union to organize Amazon workers in Alabama, indicating a long-term strategy of disruption rather than immediate unionization. The Teamsters are leveraging their local networks, community groups, and financial resources to pressure Amazon in specific municipalities across the U.S.

feed.title: AWS, NetApp unveil the first fully-managed ONTAP file system in the cloud
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) and NetApp have launched Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP, the first fully managed ONTAP file system in the cloud. This new service allows customers to run applications on AWS without altering their code or data management, leveraging NetApp's ONTAP technology traditionally used for on-premise network-attached storage (NAS). The collaboration aims to significantly expand digital transformation and AWS adoption, particularly targeting enterprises using NAS for critical applications.

feed.title: One Animation Names New Hires From Hasbro, Amazon
feed.summary: One Animation has announced two new hires to its digital team, bringing in executives from Hasbro and Amazon. Kelly Joeman joins as senior manager of audience development, focusing on amplifying the studio's digital presence and growing its 20 kids-targeted channels. Tuan Nguyen has been appointed e-commerce manager to lead the studio's direct-to-consumer e-commerce business.

feed.title: How to Set Up Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP with BlueXP Console
feed.summary: This article provides a step-by-step guide on setting up Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP using the NetApp BlueXP Console. It details the process from adding a new working environment and configuring AWS IAM credentials to defining storage capacity, creating a BlueXP Console Connector, and provisioning storage volumes. The guide highlights the benefits of FSx for ONTAP, such as multiprotocol support, high availability, and integration with other AWS services, emphasizing its utility for hybrid and multicloud environments.

feed.title: File Sharing with Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
feed.summary: This article explores the file sharing capabilities of Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP, a collaboration between AWS and NetApp. It highlights how this service simplifies system architectures for multi-region, hybrid, and multicloud deployments by offering multiprotocol file and block storage support, including NFS, SMB, and iSCSI. The piece emphasizes the integration with AWS services and NetApp's data management features, positioning FSx for ONTAP as a comprehensive data platform for digital transformation and cloud migration.

feed.title: Amazon FSx for ONTAP for Kubernetes & Containers
feed.summary: This article explores how Amazon FSx for ONTAP enhances Kubernetes and container services within the AWS ecosystem. It details FSx for ONTAP as a fully managed AWS-native service offering advanced file and block storage, addressing persistent storage challenges for containerized workloads. The article examines its integration with Amazon EKS, OpenShift/ROSA, and Amazon ECS, highlighting benefits like unified storage, data portability, and simplified operations.

feed.title: Stampede for horse med ivermectin, bogus treatment for COVID-19, steers clear of Upper Valley
feed.summary: Despite a national trend of using ivermectin, an animal dewormer, as a false treatment for COVID-19, its misuse is not widespread in the Upper Valley region. Health officials express alarm over online misinformation causing illnesses and an increase in calls to poison control centers nationwide. While local feed stores are starting to lock up ivermectin or add warning signs, some New Hampshire farmers are facing difficulties restocking the drug for their livestock due to demand, highlighting the dangers of using veterinary-grade products on humans.

feed.title: WWD: American Eagle Outfitters Logs $121 Million Profit; Acquires Logistics Company
feed.summary: American Eagle Outfitters reported a $121 million profit and acquired logistics firm Airterra to enhance its supply chain and offer faster delivery services, including same-day delivery. This strategic acquisition positions AEO to compete with major retailers like Amazon, Walmart, and Target in last-mile delivery. The company also saw strong revenue growth across its brands, particularly Aerie, and updated its operating income outlook.

feed.title: Walmart's wage bump signals pressure to raise pay in industry battle for labor
feed.summary: Walmart's decision to raise wages for 565,000 U.S. store workers by at least $1 per hour highlights the intense competition for labor in the retail industry ahead of the crucial holiday shopping season. This move, Walmart's third wage hike in a year, increases its average hourly wage to $16.40 and comes amidst a nationwide worker shortage and the impending end of federal unemployment benefits. While some workers argue the pay is still insufficient, the wage hike reflects growing pressure on retailers to offer more competitive compensation and benefits.

feed.title: JD.com’s Latest $500M Investment Is All About the Warehouse
feed.summary: JD.com's infrastructure management unit has acquired a controlling stake in China Logistics Property Holdings (CNLP) for $513 million, aiming to enhance its fulfillment capabilities across China amidst rising demand and supply chain constraints. This investment will add 65 logistics parks to JD.com's portfolio and could lead to a full takeover of CNLP, reflecting the intensifying competition in China's e-commerce sector and the strategic importance of warehouses and fulfillment centers. The acquisition aligns with JD.com's aggressive logistics investments and its broader ambition for both domestic and international expansion.

feed.title: NetApp ONTAP Storage Platform Available on AWS
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has launched Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP, a new fully managed service that allows enterprises to utilize NetApp's ONTAP data management system in the cloud. This service enables users to access all ONTAP capabilities, features, and APIs within an AWS-managed environment, facilitating hybrid cloud strategies and simplifying data migration for on-premises ONTAP users. The offering includes two storage tiers—Primary Storage for active data and Capacity Pool Storage for infrequently accessed data—and supports various access protocols and features.

feed.title: Rivian files for IPO and Goldman Sachs expands automotive portfolio with $110M investment
feed.summary: This article details Rivian's confidential IPO filing, aiming for an $80 billion valuation, and Goldman Sachs's $110 million investment in DealerPolicy, an insurance tech startup. It also covers various other significant investments and acquisitions in the automotive technology space, including deals in EV battery lifecycle management, automotive cybersecurity, and autonomous driving. The piece highlights a busy M&A environment driven by substantial undeployed private equity and venture capital.

feed.title: Amazon to open 200,000-square-foot delivery station on Northern Virginia's outskirts
feed.summary: Amazon.com Inc. is set to open a new 200,000-square-foot delivery station in Fredericksburg, Virginia, later this month. This move is part of the e-commerce giant's strategy to locate larger fulfillment centers just outside major metropolitan areas.

feed.title: Thinking outside the box: AWS signals a new era for storage
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services is projected to generate $9 billion in storage revenue in 2021, becoming the second-largest enterprise storage supplier behind Dell Technologies, and is expected to surpass $11 billion in 2022. At its third annual Storage Day, AWS announced a significant partnership with NetApp Inc. to run its full ONTAP stack natively in the cloud as a managed service, alongside other storage service enhancements. This move signals AWS's continued drive to innovate in data storage, challenging traditional on-premises models and expanding into hybrid cloud and data mesh conversations.

feed.title: EXCLUSIVE Amazon considers more proactive approach to determining what belongs on its cloud service
feed.summary: Amazon is reportedly planning to adopt a more proactive strategy to identify and remove content on its Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud platform that violates its policies, such as those against promoting violence. This initiative involves expanding the Trust & Safety team and hiring specialists to monitor for emerging threats. While an AWS spokesperson initially denied changes and stated the existing team already handles abusive content, the move suggests a potential shift towards greater content arbitration by the leading cloud provider, raising free speech debates.

feed.title: Alphabet, Tesla and Amazon are among the most favourite stocks held by US-focused mutual funds. Here are top 10 stocks
feed.summary: This article identifies Alphabet, Tesla, and Amazon as top stock holdings among U.S.-focused mutual funds available to Indian investors. It details 10 such mutual funds, highlighting their investment strategies, underlying assets, and historical performance. The funds provide Indian investors exposure to leading US companies and geographical diversification.

feed.title: Kroger eyes potential entry into Southwest Florida marketplace
feed.summary: Kroger is planning to expand its online-only grocery delivery service into Southwest Florida, eyeing Naples and Fort Myers for a new distribution "spoke" facility within the next year. This expansion would create up to 180 new jobs and intensify competition with existing grocery delivery services from Publix, Walmart, Target, and Amazon, all without building any physical stores. Kroger's strategy relies on highly automated fulfillment centers and "spoke" facilities to extend delivery reach.

feed.title: Lennox announces new integration of its iComfort thermostat
feed.summary: Lennox has expanded the compatibility of its iComfort thermostats to integrate with various smart home automation platforms, including premium systems like Control4 and popular virtual assistants such as Amazon Alexa, Apple HomeKit, Google Assistant, and IFTTT. This integration allows users to manage their Lennox heating and cooling systems through a wider range of smart home devices, offering enhanced convenience and control over their indoor environment. Control4 customers with Lennox HVAC systems and iComfort S30, E30, or M30 thermostats can request integration through their dealer, enabling centralized control from their smart home system.

feed.title: PayPal Buys Paidy in A $2.7 Billion Deal to Expand Buy Now Pay Later Service
feed.summary: PayPal Holdings Inc. announced its acquisition of Japanese buy now, pay later (BNPL) firm Paidy for 300 billion yen ($2.7 billion) to expand its presence in Japan, the world's third-largest e-commerce market. This cash deal, expected to close in Q4 2021, aims to leverage Paidy's established BNPL solutions and 6 million users to boost PayPal's position in this strategically important market. The acquisition is part of a larger trend in the payment industry, with other major players like Square and Amazon also investing heavily in BNPL services.

feed.title: Kroger says it's not trying to steal business from Publix with delivery service
feed.summary: Kroger CEO Rodney McMullen stated the company is not trying to take market share from Publix in Florida despite launching grocery delivery services in Tampa and Jacksonville, with Miami next. Kroger's strategy leverages its new high-tech distribution center in Groveland to provide seamless e-commerce service, recognizing high brand familiarity among Floridians and the shift in post-pandemic shopping habits. The company plans to expand slowly based on customer experience, with no current plans for physical stores in the state.

feed.title: DC reveals details on first WEBTOON series, a FaZe Clan comic collaboration, and more for Batman Day
feed.summary: DC has announced a host of events and releases for Batman Day on September 18th, including the debut of "Batman: Wayne Family Adventures," its first Webtoon original series. The celebrations also feature a collaboration with eSports organization FaZe Clan for an exclusive comic and the release of the scripted podcast "Batman: The Audio Adventures" on HBO Max. Additionally, DC is launching "Batman: The World," a global hardcover anthology, and offering various other comic, toy, and multimedia experiences for fans.

feed.title: Amazon EKS Anywhere launches into general availability
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services Inc. has announced the general availability of Amazon EKS Anywhere, an extension of its Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service. This new service allows customers to run Kubernetes clusters on-premises using VMware vSphere, providing an installable software package and automation tools for cluster lifecycle support. EKS Anywhere, similar to Google's Anthos and Microsoft's Azure Arc, is intended to simplify Kubernetes management across both cloud and on-premises environments, addressing the need for consistent management and deployment mechanisms for hybrid cloud operations.

feed.title: Semiconductors to account for 20% of vehicle cost by 2030
feed.summary: New research from Intel Corp. forecasts that semiconductors will constitute over 20% of the total premium vehicle bill of materials by 2030, a fivefold increase from 2019. This growth is driven by the increasing demand for semiconductors in various digital markets, including automotive, and Intel is expanding its manufacturing efforts with significant investments in new fabs to meet this demand. The company is also working to transition automotive chips to more advanced process technologies.

feed.title: Macy’s Sales Plummeted in 2020, Highlighting Pandemic’s Toll on Retail (Published 2021)
feed.summary: Macy's sales plunged 29% in 2020 to $17.3 billion, resulting in a net loss of $3.9 billion, largely due to the pandemic's impact on retail. The company is focusing on expanding its digital business, moving out of traditional malls, and testing smaller store formats to adapt to shifting consumer preferences. Macy's CEO Jeff Gennette noted the significant decline in apparel sales, particularly "dressy" clothing, and anticipates a recovery in the latter half of 2021.

feed.title: Prevent Container Risks With Advanced Container Image Sandboxing
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks announced new capabilities in Prisma Cloud's Cloud Workload Protection to enhance container security, particularly through advanced machine learning for pre-deployment image sandboxing. This allows developers and DevOps teams to analyze third-party container images for dynamic threats in an isolated environment before deployment. The update also expands host security for VMs on Azure and Google Cloud, improves web application and API security, and includes Red Hat certification for its vulnerability scanner.

feed.title: Amazon to open warehouse in Delta Township, bringing 500 jobs
feed.summary: Amazon.com, Inc. announced plans to open a 1-million-square-foot fulfillment center in Delta Township, Michigan, which will create over 500 full-time jobs. This will be Amazon's eighth fulfillment center in the state and its first in mid-Michigan, focusing on larger items. The facility is expected to be operational next year, with employees earning an average of $17 an hour plus healthcare benefits.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. (ULTA) stock rose 1.54% to $382.08 on a generally down day for the market, outperforming the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock closed $32.90 below its 52-week high and also outperformed key competitors like CVS Health Corp., Walgreens Boots Alliance Inc., and Amazon.com Inc. Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

feed.title: Will Snowflake Be Worth More Than Salesforce.com by 2030?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Snowflake could surpass Salesforce.com in market capitalization by 2030, comparing their growth rates and valuations. While Snowflake projects rapid revenue growth, its current high valuation makes it unlikely to maintain its price-to-sales ratio for a decade, suggesting Salesforce will remain more valuable. However, Snowflake's stock could still offer bigger gains due to its aggressive growth targets, despite facing more competition.

feed.title: Find Lost Items at Home with Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri
feed.summary: This article provides guidance on how to locate lost items within a home using various smart assistants such as Alexa, Google, Xfinity, and Siri. It covers different methods and tips for leveraging these technologies to find misplaced belongings.

feed.title: Alibaba-Backed Hello Inc. to Complete New Round of Financing
feed.summary: Alibaba-Backed Hello Inc. is preparing to complete a new round of financing. This development indicates ongoing investment and growth for the company, which operates in a sector of interest for major tech players like Alibaba.

feed.title: Protect Your Data with Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP
feed.summary: This article discusses how Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP helps solve complex data protection challenges in hybrid and multicloud environments. It details features like Snapshots, block-level backups, disaster recovery, and data governance with Cloud Data Sense. These capabilities enable cost-effective data management, enhanced security, and compliance with regulatory requirements.

feed.title: Tennessee panel OKs $65M in state incentives for Oracle
feed.summary: A Tennessee panel has approved $65 million in state incentives for Oracle Corp. The company plans to create 8,500 jobs and invest $1.2 billion in Nashville over the next decade, with an average salary of $110,000. This expansion would surpass Amazon's previous investment in the city.

feed.title: UPS to hire over 100k essential seasonal workers
feed.summary: UPS announced its plan to hire over 100,000 essential seasonal workers to manage the anticipated increase in package volume during the 2021 holiday season, which runs from October 2021 through January 2022. The company aims for a quick hiring process, with many qualified candidates receiving an offer within 30 minutes of applying. UPS also highlighted its track record of converting seasonal roles into permanent positions, with nearly a third of its current U.S. workforce having started in seasonal jobs.

feed.title: Apple can’t force developers to use its in-app payment system, federal judge rules
feed.summary: A federal judge ruled that Apple Inc. can no longer compel developers to use its in-app payment system, allowing them to bypass commission fees of 15% to 30%. This decision, stemming from the Epic Games Inc. case, could significantly alter how apps are distributed by dominant online platforms like Apple, Alphabet Inc. (Google), and Amazon.com Inc. However, the ruling stopped short of declaring Apple an antitrust monopolist.

feed.title: How Dutch grocery giant Ahold Delhaize is taking on U.S. rivals with tech
feed.summary: Ahold Delhaize, a Dutch grocery conglomerate, is leveraging technology and strategic acquisitions to compete with major U.S. rivals like Kroger, Walmart, and Albertsons, especially in the online grocery market. The company, which operates brands like Stop & Shop and Food Lion in the U.S., is investing in automated distribution centers, launching new e-commerce platforms like Ship2Me, and focusing on organic growth and smaller urban stores. CEO Frans Muller believes hybrid fulfillment models and catering to changed consumer habits post-pandemic, such as increased home cooking, will sustain their growth.

feed.title: “We are not giving up.” Alabama Coal Miners Enter Sixth Month of Strike
feed.summary: Alabama coal miners at Warrior Met Coal Inc. have entered their sixth month of a historic strike, fighting for better wages, reasonable hours, and improved safety conditions against a company that declared bankruptcy and then exploited its workers. The article details the harsh working conditions, the workers' personal sacrifices, and the political complexities and contradictions encountered during the prolonged labor dispute, highlighting the efforts by the Left Voice to support and amplify their message. Despite challenges and limited support from mainstream media and politicians, the miners remain determined, with solidarity efforts extending to other labor movements, including efforts by a Women's Auxiliary Committee.

feed.title: Naturally Network to Launch Officially at Expo East
feed.summary: Naturally Network, a new national trade association for the natural and organic product industry, will officially launch at Natural Products Expo East. Founded in June, the 501(c)(6) business association aims to foster growth and support local affiliates, members, and sponsors across the country. Executive Director Sharon Reddehase will lead the organization, which has already established a board of directors and secured founding sponsors through its Founders Circle.

feed.title: Single Stocks & ETFs Do Mix
feed.summary: This article discusses how single stocks and ETFs can both have a place in an investment portfolio. It highlights the similarities and differences, emphasizing the importance of considering overall portfolio concentration when adding individual stocks. The author also weighs the pros and cons, such as lower costs for single stocks versus the diversification and professional management benefits of ETFs.

feed.title: Apple, Google, Amazon spying on you, lawsuits claim
feed.summary: Lawsuits allege that Apple, Google, and Amazon are spying on users through their voice assistants, recording private conversations and sharing them with advertisers. A class-action lawsuit against Apple's Siri has been greenlit by a federal judge, with similar claims made against Google and Amazon.
