The companies deny these allegations, stating that recordings are only kept with user consent or to improve service, and offer options for users to manage their privacy settings.

feed.title: Copado raises $140 million on $1.2B valuation to expand its DevOps platform
feed.summary: Copado, a DevOps platform for Salesforce, has secured $140 million in Series C funding, led by Insight Partners, pushing its valuation to nearly $1.2 billion. The company plans to use the new capital to expand its DevOps and AI-driven testing solutions across various enterprise SaaS platforms and accelerate international growth, especially in the Asia-Pacific region. Copado, founded in 2013, initially focused on Salesforce but has since expanded to support other cloud platforms and has acquired DevSecOps and AI-testing companies.

feed.title: GetYourGuide gets help from Expedia, Amazon widens virtual tours
feed.summary: Expedia has partnered with GetYourGuide to distribute tours and activities, allowing customers to book GetYourGuide's offerings directly on Expedia's sites. This move follows Expedia's shift to sourcing third-party inventory after closing its own concierge business. Simultaneously, Amazon has expanded its virtual tours service, Amazon Explore, to include small group experiences, enabling multiple customers from different locations to participate in live virtual activities.

feed.title: Cloudflare Workers: the Fast Serverless Platform
feed.summary: This article discusses Cloudflare Workers, a serverless platform designed for speed and efficiency at the edge. It highlights how Workers significantly outperform other serverless offerings like Lambda and Lambda@Edge in terms of speed and addresses the challenges posed by traditional container-based serverless architectures by leveraging lightweight V8 isolates. The post emphasizes that Workers' architecture eliminates cold starts and provides a better developer and end-user experience due to its global edge network and continuous performance improvements.

feed.title: Redesigned Sonos Beam supports Dolby Atmos, eARC
feed.summary: Sonos has redesigned its Beam soundbar, now in its second generation, to include Dolby Atmos support and HDMI eARC connectivity. The newer model retains the compact size of its predecessor but features a faster CPU for virtualized Dolby Atmos height effects and an updated polycarbonate grille. Shipping October 5 for $449, it will also support lossless and Dolby Atmos Music from Amazon Music.

feed.title: Kroger and Instacart launch 30-minute grocery delivery service in the U.S.
feed.summary: Kroger and Instacart have partnered to launch "Kroger Delivery Now," a new home-delivery service promising groceries and household staples in as little as 30 minutes. This offering aims to reach up to 50 million U.S. households, significantly expanding Kroger's e-commerce presence amid competition from Walmart and Amazon. The service emphasizes convenience, providing quick delivery of 25,000 items through Kroger's platforms and a dedicated "Convenience Hub" on Instacart.

feed.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste - ASR - Stock Price & News
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive overview of Grupo Aeroportuario del Sureste (ASR) stock, including its current price, key data points, return performance against the S&P, company information, and recent news and analysis. It also presents detailed financial health metrics, valuation ratios, and earnings call transcripts for investors.

feed.title: Spotify Rival Anghami partners with Amazon to give Prime members in Saudi Arabia and UAE 6 months Anghami Plus for free
feed.summary: Anghami, a music streaming company focused on the Middle East and North Africa, has teamed up with Amazon to provide Prime members in Saudi Arabia and the UAE with a free six-month subscription to its premium service, Anghami Plus. This collaboration will be followed by a 50% discount for the subsequent six months. The partnership aims to offer Prime members access to millions of songs, podcasts, and unlimited downloads, enhancing their entertainment benefits in regions where Amazon Music Unlimited is not currently available.

feed.title: Intuit (INTU) to Buy Digital Marketing Firm Mailchimp for $12B
feed.summary: Intuit (INTU) announced its agreement to acquire digital marketing firm Mailchimp for $12 billion in a cash-and-stock deal, expected to close by the end of fiscal second-quarter 2022. This acquisition aims to boost Intuit's small-business capabilities and enhance its customer reach by integrating Mailchimp’s email marketing services and AI-driven technology with Intuit’s QuickBooks platform. The deal is intended to provide an end-to-end customer growth platform for small and mid-market businesses and is anticipated to be accretive to Intuit’s fiscal 2022 adjusted earnings.

feed.title: The Sonos Beam Gen 2 Is Now Available | WiFiHiFi
feed.summary: The Sonos Beam Gen 2 is a new compact soundbar with enhanced processing power, supporting Dolby Atmos and Ultra HD audio. Available for pre-order at $559 in Canada, it features a faster CPU, re-tuned speaker arrays for crisper dialogue, and eARC compatibility for a more immersive sound experience. Sonos also plans to support Amazon Music's Ultra High Definition audio and Dolby Atmos Music on various Sonos speakers, while the Beam Gen 2 introduces a new polycarbonate grille and NFC for easier setup.

feed.title: Amazon hikes average U.S. starting pay to $18, hires for 125,000 jobs
feed.summary: Amazon has increased its average U.S. starting wage to over $18 an hour and plans to hire an additional 125,000 warehouse and transportation workers. This move comes amidst a tight U.S. labor market and increased scrutiny of Amazon's labor practices. The company aims to fill these roles quickly to support its continued growth and enhance its logistics capabilities, including its one-day delivery service for Prime members.

feed.title: Procore Appoints Sandra Benson as Global Head of Industry Transformation
feed.summary: Procore Technologies, Inc. has appointed construction technology expert Sandra Benson as its Global Head of Industry Transformation. In this new role, Benson will advise Procore’s product and sales engineering teams on customer and partner requirements, leveraging her extensive experience in the building and construction technology industry. She is also a presidential appointee to the Board of Directors of the National Institute of Building Sciences (NIBS).

feed.title: SAP and Amazon Business Partner to Expand Buyer Choice
feed.summary: SAP and Amazon Business have partnered to integrate Amazon Business into SAP Ariba solutions, allowing employees to purchase from Amazon Business directly within SAP Ariba. This collaboration aims to expand buyer choice and simplify procurement for general business goods, leveraging a real-time search API and extending to the Concur Expense solution. The integration is set to roll out globally, beginning with early adopters in the United States.

feed.title: After Brazil, Shopee tops rankings in Mexico, Colombia and Chile - now available to 1B people
feed.summary: Shopee has expanded its dominance in Latin America, becoming the most downloaded shopping app in Mexico, Colombia, and Chile, mirroring its success in Brazil. Parent company Sea Ltd. secured an additional US$6 billion in funding to fuel aggressive market expansion. Shopee is also planning to enter the European market, starting with Poland, seeking to replicate its developing-world success in more established economies.

feed.title: Real Estate Roundup: North Phoenix office building sells for $22M and 10 other deals to know
feed.summary: This article provides a roundup of recent real estate transactions and store openings in the Valley area. It highlights the sale of a North Phoenix office building for $22 million, an apartment sale in Tempe, a surgery center sale in Chandler, and a land sale in Surprise. The report covers nearly a dozen significant real estate deals and new establishments across the region.

feed.title: Kendall Jenner Was Influenced By Instagram To Buy Her Camo Crocs
feed.summary: Kendall Jenner appeared on The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon and revealed that she bought her "controversial" camo Crocs due to Instagram influencing her. She saw them on a fashion inspiration page and found them "too cute." This comes as Crocs sees a surge in popularity and anticipates doubling its revenue to over $5 billion by 2026.

feed.title: Need A Job? Amazon Hiring Thousands Of Employees Including Michig
feed.summary: Amazon is looking to hire 125,000 employees across the country, including in Michigan, with pay ranging from $18 to $22.50 per hour. The company offers comprehensive benefits, including health, vision, and dental insurance, 401(k) with company match, paid parental leave, and full college tuition for front-line employees through its Career Choice program. Interested applicants can find open positions and apply at hiring.amazon.com.

feed.title: Calavo Growers appoints Amazon executive as CFO
feed.summary: Calavo Growers, Inc. announced the appointment of Mariela Matute as Chief Financial Officer, effective October 11, 2021. Matute previously served as Director and CFO for Amazon Business, bringing extensive financial expertise and relevant industry background to Calavo. The company expects her leadership to help execute its strategic plan, accelerate profitable growth, and enhance shareholder value.

feed.title: O'Reilly Automotive - ORLY - Stock Price & News
feed.summary: This page provides an overview of O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY), including its current stock price, market capitalization, and key financial metrics such as revenue, net income, and EPS. It also lists recent news articles related to the company and provides details about its industry, sector, and leadership. The page highlights essential performance indicators and financial health at a glance for investors.

feed.title: Carvana CEO’s dad goes on $3.6B stock selling spree, raising eyebrows
feed.summary: Carvana CEO's father, Ernie Garcia II, has sold over $3.6 billion in company stock since October, raising concerns about the family's control over the used car dealership. This selling spree, facilitated by a predetermined plan that was modified twice, follows a period where Garcia II and his son bought shares at "bargain basement" prices during the pandemic, leading to a lawsuit for insider trading. Despite the massive sales, Garcia II still holds a significant stake, with the company's annual report even warning that the Garcia family's interests might not always align with other stockholders.

feed.title: Corsair Sabre RGB Pro Wireless gaming mouse review
feed.summary: The Corsair Sabre RGB Pro Wireless gaming mouse offers an ultra-light build, strong performance, and freedom of movement, despite being slightly less fast than its wired counterpart. It features 2K polling, 26K DPI, and customizable settings through the robust iCue software, making it an affordable and versatile option for most gamers.

feed.title: Highly Immersive Phygital Malls
feed.summary: JD.com has launched a massive 42,000-square-meter "phygital" shopping mall in Xi'an, China, aiming to blend physical and digital retail experiences. The mall features five floors with over 200,000 items from 150 brands, allowing shoppers to scan QR codes for home delivery. It also incorporates interactive technology like holographic projections, VR equipment, and AI robots to enhance the immersive experience.

feed.title: Salesforce values and culture driven innovation
feed.summary: This article explores how Salesforce has evolved from a CRM company to a leading cloud and productivity organization, driven by its core values and culture. Field CTO Adam Spearing discusses how the company's focus has shifted towards digital transformation, accelerated by the pandemic, and highlights key acquisitions like Tableau and Slack. The piece emphasizes the importance of culture, customer-centric innovation, and collaborative ecosystems in Salesforce's strategy for future growth.

feed.title: WSJ News Exclusive | CEO’s Father Gets a $3.6 Billion Stock Windfall at Carvana
feed.summary: Ernie Garcia II, father of Carvana Co.'s CEO, has sold over $3.6 billion in Carvana stock since October, representing 16% of his holdings. This windfall is attributed to a corporate ownership structure that benefits the family and allows them to maintain control, though it may be at the expense of other shareholders. The article highlights how his stock sales rank him among the top individuals for earnings from company stock sales in the past year, excluding prominent tech and retail magnates.

feed.title: Is a stock market correction on the horizon?
feed.summary: The S&P 500 has seen a recent dip, leading analysts to narrow predictions for the U.S. equity market. While tech stocks may face challenges due to Fed policy and rising interest rates, other sectors like energy and small-caps are showing potential. Despite concerns, corrections are normal, and many analysts remain bullish on long-term market growth, suggesting current dips could be buying opportunities.

feed.title: 39 Billion by 2026: America’s Carriers Will Nearly Double Their Package Hauls, Data Shows
feed.summary: A Pitney Bowes report indicates U.S. parcel volume surged 37% in 2020 to 20 billion, driven by e-commerce due to the pandemic. The study forecasts a near doubling of parcel volume to 32-39 billion by 2026, with a 10% CAGR. Major carriers like UPS, FedEx, and USPS saw significant revenue growth, while Amazon Logistics rapidly increased its market share.

feed.title: How GoodRx Fended Off Amazon
feed.summary: GoodRx, a prescription drug comparison app, has successfully thrived despite competition from Amazon's prescription drug discount program. The company has seen significant growth in revenue and customer numbers, driven by a rebound in prescription volumes and strategic diversification into telehealth and other health technology solutions. GoodRx plans further expansion into complementary markets to address various consumer pain points in healthcare.

feed.title: The Climate Pledge announces 86 new signatories
feed.summary: The Climate Pledge, co-founded by Amazon and Global Optimism, has announced 86 new signatories, bringing the total to over 201 companies committed to achieving net-zero carbon by 2040, ten years ahead of the Paris Agreement. These new additions, including major businesses like Nespresso, ASOS, Procter & Gamble, HP, and Salesforce, represent various industries across 21 countries and are collectively expected to significantly mitigate carbon emissions. The initiative emphasizes collaboration and real business changes in response to the urgent climate crisis highlighted by recent IPCC reports.

feed.title: Mattel, Warner Music Group's Arts Music Release He-Man, Hot Wheels, Thomas & Friends Albums
feed.summary: Mattel has partnered with Warner Music Group's Arts Music division to release new albums based on popular franchises like He-Man and the Masters of the Universe, Hot Wheels, and Thomas & Friends. These releases build upon an existing multi-year global music licensing agreement. The new albums feature original soundtracks and theme songs from their respective series, with additional releases planned for the future across major digital music platforms.

feed.title: Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP – The easiest storage decision you’ll ever make
feed.summary: This article argues that Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP is the easiest storage decision for businesses, highlighting its simplicity, built-in technology, and security. The author contrasts FSx for ONTAP's quick setup and transparent pricing with the complexity, high costs, and longer commitment periods associated with Dell's Cloud Storage Services and Pure Storage's Cloud Block Store. The piece emphasizes FSx for ONTAP's native AWS integration and comprehensive features, positioning it as a superior and more cost-effective solution for hybrid cloud storage.

feed.title: The Climate Pledge Announces 86 New Signatories, Including Procter & Gamble, HP, and Salesforce, Signaling Their Commitment to Achieving Net-Zero Carbon by 2040 or Sooner.
feed.summary: The Climate Pledge has announced 86 new signatories, including major companies like Procter & Gamble, HP, and Salesforce, bringing the total to over 200 global organizations. These companies commit to achieving net-zero carbon by 2040, a decade ahead of the Paris Agreement, and are collectively expected to mitigate 1.98 billion metric tons of carbon emissions annually by 2040. The announcement emphasizes the urgency of collective action by businesses to address climate change based on recent IPCC findings.

feed.title: Lionsgate A Juicy M&A Target, Vice Chair Michael Burns Agrees
feed.summary: Lionsgate Vice Chairman Michael Burns acknowledged the company is a prime M&A target amid media industry consolidation, stating that its goal is to increase stock value. He highlighted Lionsgate's nimbleness and extensive library as key advantages, especially in the streaming era. Burns also commented on the evolving landscape of talent deals and theatrical windows, describing it as the "Wild West."

feed.title: Lionsgate Vice Chair: “There’s Great Scarcity Value” in Being a Major Indie Studio Now
feed.summary: Lionsgate vice chairman Michael Burns believes the Hollywood studio is an attractive takeover target due to its status as a large independent company amidst ongoing industry consolidation and the streaming boom. He highlights the "scarcity value" of independent studios and Lionsgate's strategy of acquiring content libraries while others focus on mergers. Burns also discussed the evolving landscape of talent compensation as films increasingly move directly to streaming.

feed.title: Rackspace Launches Rackspace Data Freedom to Eliminate Cloud Lock-In
feed.summary: Rackspace Technology has introduced Rackspace Data Freedom, a new platform designed to help organizations avoid cloud lock-in by separating storage from public cloud compute. This service aims to eliminate unpredictable data egress charges, allowing customers to save on costs and maintain data sovereignty across multi-cloud environments. Key benefits include significant cost reduction through Rackspace's private network, enhanced data accessibility via RackConnect Global, and customized storage solutions to meet specific user requirements.

feed.title: Kilroy Pays $490M for Amazon-Anchored Tower
feed.summary: Kilroy Realty Corp. acquired West 8th, a 539,000-square-foot, LEED Platinum certified office tower in Seattle, for $490 million. The building is fully leased, with Amazon occupying approximately 70 percent of the space, and was previously purchased by DWS Group in 2016 for $370 million. This acquisition expands Kilroy's Seattle footprint and follows other significant investments and a disposition by the REIT in Texas and San Francisco.

feed.title: BSX Begins Publishing MIAX Pearl Equities
feed.summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX), a subsidiary of Miami International Holdings, Inc., has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for 10 actively traded U.S. companies to the Pyth Network. This marks BSX's first initiative to provide high-fidelity data to the Pyth Network, contributing U.S. equities exchange market data to support DeFi markets. The initial companies include Apple, Amazon, Google, Netflix, and Tesla.

feed.title: FedEx labor shortfall hits quarterly profit, earnings forecast
feed.summary: FedEx (FDX.N) reported a 7% drop in quarterly profit and lowered its full-year earnings forecast due to labor shortages, which led to increased costs and slowed package delivery. The company incurred an extra $450 million in costs from higher wages, overtime, and third-party transportation, particularly affecting its Ground network which is now rerouting 600,000 packages daily. FedEx plans to hire 90,000 holiday workers to address staffing issues as it prepares for the peak season.

feed.title: BSX publishes market data to Pyth Network
feed.summary: The Bermuda Stock Exchange (BSX) has started publishing MIAX Pearl Equities market data for ten US companies to the Pyth Network, a decentralized financial market data distribution platform. This initiative marks BSX's first step in providing high-fidelity data to the blockchain industry via the Solana network, with plans to expand the data breadth. The included companies are major US firms like Apple, Amazon, and Tesla, marking a significant development for DeFi markets.

feed.title: Netflix Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Shares of Netflix Inc. (NFLX) rose 3.06% to $590.65 on Wednesday, outperforming the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average. The stock's performance also beat out some competitors like Amazon.com Inc. (AMZN) and Walt Disney Co. (DIS), although Comcast Corp. Cl A (CMCSA) saw a slight dip. This rise snapped a two-day losing streak for Netflix.

feed.title: Brian Roberts: Comcast is ‘Really a Broadband Company’
feed.summary: Comcast chairman/CEO Brian Roberts stated that Comcast has transformed into a broadband company, having accumulated 33 million customers and adding about 1 million broadband subscribers annually for two decades. This growth greatly offsets declines in cable TV subscriptions. Roberts emphasized the company's focus on deepening broadband relationships through enhanced connectivity and new devices like XiOne, positioning Comcast as a leader in the evolving high-speed Internet landscape.

feed.title: The Market Remains Hot As We Kick Off Our Next Forrester Wave™ Evaluation Of The DAM Landscape
feed.summary: Forrester is launching its next Wave evaluation for Digital Asset Management (DAM) due to significant market growth and increasing customer demand. Many organizations struggle with content creation challenges, such as outdated processes, content volume outpacing production capabilities, and the need for better personalization foundations. DAM solutions offer governance, centralization, metadata enrichment, content atomization, and AI-powered tagging, distinguishing them from basic cloud storage options.

feed.title: General Motors and Amazon Team Up to Offer OnStar Emergency Service at Home
feed.summary: General Motors and Amazon have partnered to introduce the OnStar Guardian skill for Amazon Alexa, enabling users to access OnStar's Emergency-Certified Advisors at home through Alexa-enabled devices. This new feature allows family members to call for help in various crisis situations, extending OnStar's safety and security services beyond the vehicle to in-home environments. The service will initially roll out to select OnStar members, with a broader release planned for 2022.

feed.title: Kilroy Realty buys Amazon-leased tower in Seattle's Denny Triangle for $490M
feed.summary: Kilroy Realty, a Los Angeles-based real estate firm, has acquired an Amazon-leased tower in Seattle's Denny Triangle for $490 million. This acquisition signals Kilroy Realty's confidence in major tenants eventually returning to physical offices, despite the ongoing uncertainty surrounding office reopening plans. The purchase highlights the continued investment interest in Seattle's commercial real estate market, especially for properties anchored by large tech companies.

feed.title: Iconix Brand Group Steps Into The Footwear Market With New Licensee for Starter
feed.summary: Iconix Brand Group has signed a three-year licensing agreement with Genesco Inc. to make them the exclusive US and Canadian footwear licensee for STARTER. This deal marks Starter’s return to footwear in the U.S. and Canada after a five-year absence, with a collection of approximately 30 SKUs planned to launch in Spring 2022. The partnership aims to capitalize on the growing consumer demand for heritage brands and expand STARTER's presence in the athletic footwear market.

feed.title: Cisco Named a Leader in 2021 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for WAN Edge Infrastructure
feed.summary: Cisco has announced its recognition as a Leader in the 2021 Gartner Magic Quadrant for WAN Edge Infrastructure, highlighting its SD-WAN solutions. These solutions focus on enabling secure, predictable digital experiences for hybrid workforces, building dynamic workspaces, and securing multi-cloud environments, accelerating the transition to a SASE-enabled architecture. Cisco emphasizes its comprehensive capabilities in seamless multicloud access, integrated security, and optimized insights through ThousandEyes integration.

feed.title: Autonomous pilot program tests Paccar-built FedEx trucks in Texas
feed.summary: A new pilot program is testing autonomous Paccar-built FedEx trucks in Texas, utilizing self-driving technology from an Amazon-backed startup. These trucks will operate with a safety driver, autonomously hauling FedEx loads on a 500-mile round trip between Dallas and Houston. The initiative highlights ongoing advancements and investments in autonomous vehicle technology for commercial logistics.

feed.title: Amazon's cloud unit to create data centres, 1,000 jobs in New Zealand
feed.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) announced plans to launch data centres in New Zealand by 2024, investing NZ$7.5 billion ($5.25 billion) in a new infrastructure hub. This initiative is expected to create approximately 1,000 jobs in the country over 15 years. The new hub will allow developers, startups, and government entities to use local data centres, fostering digital transformation and economic growth.

feed.title: Samsung to make Tesla’s HW 4.0 self-driving auto chip
feed.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. has won the contract to manufacture Tesla Inc.'s next-generation hardware 4 (HW 4.0) chip for its autonomous driving technology, beating out TSMC. Samsung plans to mass-produce the HW 4.0 chip using 7-nanometer processing technology, ensuring higher production yields and stable functions for Tesla's upcoming vehicles like the Cybertruck. This partnership strengthens Samsung's position in the global foundry market as it competes with TSMC and expands its collaborations with other tech giants like Google and Amazon.

feed.title: If You Missed the PS5 Restock, Here’s How to Prepare for the Next One
feed.summary: This article discusses the ongoing PS5 stock shortages, nearly a year after its release, exacerbated by the pandemic and chip shortages. It offers tips for securing a console during future restocks, such as signing up for retailer accounts with current billing/shipping info, avoiding PayPal for faster checkout, and following Twitter alert accounts like @PS5StockAlerts, @mattswider, and @Wario64. The piece highlights that despite recent rumors of a major restock, consoles quickly sold out.

feed.title: Barclays announces new CEO of point-of-sale finance business
feed.summary: Barclays has appointed Antony Stephen as the new CEO of its point-of-sale finance business, Barclays Partner Finance, effective October 4, 2021. Stephen, who previously served as Head of Amazon Payments Europe, will focus on expanding the UK point-of-sale finance business and providing leadership for Barclays Cubed, the bank's next-generation commerce platform. His extensive experience in payments and financial services is expected to drive growth and innovation in the rapidly evolving point-of-sale finance market.

feed.title: Daxko
feed.summary: Daxko, a SaaS platform for fitness center management, faced significant challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic due to its customers being heavily impacted. To manage rapid changes, integrate nine acquisitions, and provide crucial support to its clients, Daxko implemented IBM Instana Observability. This allowed them to quickly adapt their product roadmap, gain comprehensive visibility into their complex IT environment, and minimize downtime, ultimately helping their customers navigate the unprecedented times.

feed.title: Starbucks pushes back against US workers’ cafe-by-cafe union vote
authors: Amelia Pollard and Josh Eidelson and Bloomberg
feed.summary: Starbucks is opposing unionization efforts by workers in Buffalo, New York, arguing that any union vote should encompass all 20 of its Buffalo-area locations rather than individual cafes. The company's stance challenges a labor board precedent that typically favors smaller bargaining units, with a hearing officer noting that single-location units are "presumptively appropriate." This move by Starbucks aligns with employer preferences for larger units to make unionization more difficult, amidst a broader wave of labor activism at nonunion companies.

feed.title: Viral TikTok video accuses Zillow and others of manipulating the housing market. Here’s what’s really going on
feed.summary: A viral TikTok video by Nevada real-estate agent Sean Gotcher claims that companies like Zillow and Redfin are manipulating home prices through their "iBuying" business model. However, real-estate experts suggest the situation is more complex, and a professor noted that if the residential housing market could be rigged so easily, realtors would have done so long ago. The core of the accusation is that a company uses search data to strategically purchase homes in specific ZIP codes.

feed.title: Avantis U.S. Large Cap Value ETF
feed.summary: The Avantis U.S. Large Cap Value ETF (AVLV) offers investors exposure to large-cap U.S. value equities with an active management strategy. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.15% and has shown strong performance across various timeframes compared to its category average. It holds a diversified portfolio with significant allocations to finance, retail trade, and electronic technology sectors, and its top holdings include major tech and industrial companies.

feed.title: Intel breaks ground on $20 bln Arizona plants as U.S. chip factory race heats up
feed.summary: Intel has broken ground on two new $20 billion chip factories in Arizona, named Fab 52 and Fab 62, as part of its strategy to regain leadership in advanced chip manufacturing and become a major producer for external customers. These plants will be crucial for Intel's goal to lead in chip technology by 2025 and will bolster the U.S. chip supply chain. CEO Pat Gelsinger noted the facilities will also serve outside companies like Qualcomm and Amazon, and Intel plans a new U.S. campus with additional factories by year-end.
